Reviewing 0a10e42 on NMP master: T31 m105-followup-1 self-driven round-3 addressing codex round-2 findings from review 8660961.md.

NMP doctrine D0-D8 per docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md section 1.5. File size 300 soft / 500 hard.

T31 round-2 codex (8660961.md) had 3 P1 findings:
P1.a S3/S4/S5 use synthetic read-cache events not VerifiedEvent + EventStore::insert
P1.b Gates weakened vs spec: S1 60% not 90%, S2 98% not 100%, S3 1k not 100k events, S4 stale-rev gates missing
P1.c S5 watchdog timing broken (Instant::elapsed() == 0)

Verify which are addressed in 0a10e42. The commit message names S5 epoch watchdog + S4 stall-liveness gate + S1 deferred note — suggests P1.c done, P1.b partial, P1.a maybe untouched.

For each, name file:line + status (FIXED / PARTIAL / NOT ADDRESSED + why).

DIFF FOLLOWS:
=== 0a10e42 (T31 self-driven round-3) ===
 .../nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s1_mount_unmount.rs |  3 +-
 .../bin/ffi-stress/s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs   | 49 +++++++-----
 crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s5_reentrancy.rs | 92 ++++++++++++----------
 3 files changed, 85 insertions(+), 59 deletions(-)

0a10e42 fix(m10.5): codex follow-up — S5 epoch watchdog + S4 stall-liveness gate + S1 deferred note
S5: replace broken t0.elapsed() ≈ 0 watchdog with shared OnceLock<Instant>
epoch. CB_IN_FLIGHT_TS_MS=0 means idle; non-zero stores epoch-relative entry
ms. Watchdog fires when (epoch.elapsed_ms - entry_ms) > 5000 — correctly
measures real wall-time the callback has been in flight.

S4: replace Gate::eq("dropped_emits", 0.0, 0.0) no-op with meaningful
stall_windows_starved gate (counts stall windows where zero emits arrived,
indicating the actor was blocked by the sleeping callback). Remove
hardcoded apply_burst_ms = 0.0 gate; defer to phase-2 with explicit note
in docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md §G-S4.

S1: add explicit DEFERRED comment on 90% cycles threshold — phase-2
XCUITest device execution target; not achievable via macOS sim sleep(1ms).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

     // Net heap slope (bytes/sec) in steady state — D8 invariant.

@@ -193,30 +188,47 @@ pub(crate) fn run(cfg: S4Config, report: &mut ScenarioMetrics) {

-    // Stale-rev drops: spec expects >= 1 stale-rev event per stall (buffered emits

-        Gate::eq("dropped_emits", 0.0, 0.0)

+    // Stall liveness: the actor must continue emitting while the callback is blocked.
+    // Each stall window is at least stall_ms (250 ms) + 50 ms margin; with emit_hz=4
+    // that yields at least 1 emit per stall.  We count stall windows where zero emits
+    // arrived — a non-zero count means the actor was blocked (backpressure failure).
+    let total_stall_backlog: u64 = stall_pre_counts
+        .iter()
+        .zip(stall_post_counts.iter())
+        .map(|(pre, post)| post.saturating_sub(*pre))
+        .sum();
+    let stall_windows_starved: u64 = stall_pre_counts
+        .iter()
+        .zip(stall_post_counts.iter())
+        .filter(|(pre, post)| *post <= *pre)
+        .count() as u64;
     report.gates.push(
-        Gate::lte("apply_burst_ms", apply_burst_ms, 33.0)
-            .with_note("G-S4: apply-after-resume burst <= 33 ms (2 frames)"),
+        Gate::eq("stall_windows_starved", stall_windows_starved as f64, 0.0)
+            .with_note(
+                "G-S4: stall windows with zero emits == 0 \
+                 (actor not blocked by sleeping callback)",
+            ),
     );
+    // Apply-after-resume burst: actor-tick tracing is not available from the FFI side.
+    // Phase-2 deliverable: instrument actor with emit-timing telemetry (tracked in
+    // docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md §G-S4 TODO).  Gate omitted this phase.
 
     report.notes.push(format!(
         "Injected {} events; stalls: {}; max backlog: {}; expected <= {}; \
-         emits total: {}; stale-rev pairs: {}",
+         emits total: {}; stale-rev pairs: {}; total_stall_backlog: {}; starved_windows: {}",
         cfg.inject_count, stalls_injected, max_backlog_emits, expected_max,
-        emit_count, stale_rev_pairs
+        emit_count, stale_rev_pairs, total_stall_backlog, stall_windows_starved
     ));
     report.notes.push(
         "Stall simulated via callback sleep (250 ms).  Actor is not blocked; \
          configure() returns immediately (D4 single-writer via actor thread)."
             .to_string(),
     );
+    report.notes.push(
+        "apply_burst_ms gate deferred to phase-2 (needs actor-tick telemetry). \
+         See docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md §G-S4."
+            .to_string(),
+    );
 
     report.measurements = json!({
         "inject_count": cfg.inject_count,
@@ -225,10 +237,11 @@ pub(crate) fn run(cfg: S4Config, report: &mut ScenarioMetrics) {
         "emit_hz": cfg.emit_hz,
         "max_backlog_emits": max_backlog_emits,

-/// Shared timestamp: updated by the callback on entry, read by the watchdog.

+/// Shared epoch: set once at scenario start; all timestamps are ms-since-epoch.
+/// 0 in CB_IN_FLIGHT_TS_MS means no callback is in flight.
+static EPOCH: OnceLock<Instant> = OnceLock::new();
+/// Non-zero while a callback is in flight: stores epoch-relative ms of callback entry.
+/// Reset to 0 on callback return.  Watchdog: fires if non-zero AND age > WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS.
+static CB_IN_FLIGHT_TS_MS: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
+
 static EMIT_COUNT: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
 static REENTRANT_DISPATCHES: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
-/// Set by callback on RETURN; watchdog compares with LAST_CB_ENTRY_MS.
-static LAST_CB_RETURN_MS: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
 /// Set by watchdog if a deadlock is detected; scenario treats as fatal.
 static WATCHDOG_FIRED: AtomicBool = AtomicBool::new(false);
 /// Set by run() when the scenario loop finishes; watchdog exits on this.

     let emit_n = EMIT_COUNT.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);

     inject_events(app, "s5-", 1_700_000_000, cfg.inject_count);
 
     // Spawn external watchdog BEFORE the dispatch loop.
-    // The watchdog monitors the gap between LAST_CB_ENTRY_MS and LAST_CB_RETURN_MS.
-    // If an emit callback has been running for > WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS without
-    // returning, the scenario is deadlocked; the watchdog writes metrics and
-    // terminates the process.
+    //
+    // Protocol:
+    //   - CB_IN_FLIGHT_TS_MS == 0  → no callback in flight (safe).
+    //   - CB_IN_FLIGHT_TS_MS > 0   → callback entered at that epoch-ms; watchdog fires
+    //                                 if (epoch.elapsed_ms - stored_ms) > WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS.
+    //
+    // This is correct because EPOCH is monotonic and shared between callback and watchdog;
+    // the gap is the true wall-time the callback has been running.
     let watchdog = std::thread::spawn(move || {
         loop {
             if SCENARIO_DONE.load(Ordering::Acquire) {
                 break;
             }
             std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(500));
-            let entry_ms = LAST_CB_ENTRY_MS.load(Ordering::Acquire);
-            let return_ms = LAST_CB_RETURN_MS.load(Ordering::Acquire);
-            // Callback is "in flight" when entry_ms > return_ms.
-            if entry_ms > return_ms {
-                let gap = entry_ms.saturating_sub(return_ms);
-                if gap > WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS {
-                    WATCHDOG_FIRED.store(true, Ordering::Release);
-                    eprintln!(

-                         (threshold {WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS} ms). Terminating."
-                    );
-                    std::process::exit(1);
-                }
+            let in_flight_ms = CB_IN_FLIGHT_TS_MS.load(Ordering::Acquire);
+            if in_flight_ms == 0 {
+                continue; // no callback in flight
+            }
+            let now_ms = EPOCH
+                .get()
+                .map(|e| e.elapsed().as_millis() as u64)
+                .unwrap_or(0);
+            let age_ms = now_ms.saturating_sub(in_flight_ms);
+            if age_ms > WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS {
+                WATCHDOG_FIRED.store(true, Ordering::Release);
+                eprintln!(
+                    "ffi-stress S5: DEADLOCK detected — callback in flight for {age_ms} ms \
+                     (threshold {WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS} ms). Terminating."
+                );
+                std::process::exit(1);
             }
         }
     });

crates/nmp-testing/tests/m2_p_tag_inbox_routing.rs:11://! Doctrine: D3 (outbox routing automatic).
docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:181:- **Best-effort rendering.** Doctrine D1: render what's available, refine in place; never withhold cached data; never block on fetches.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:1:# Cardinal Doctrines D0–D8
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:7:**Two kinds of doctrine.** D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines — they govern user-facing semantics (what the framework promises, what it forbids). D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* — they govern how the runtime is allowed to be implemented (what crosses FFI, how state changes propagate, what the hot path can do). Both kinds are equally binding; their distinction is the kind of review they enforce. Policy review flags "this API choice violates a user-facing principle"; substrate review flags "this implementation choice will leak across FFI / hide policy on the native side / degrade reactivity."
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:9:## D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:21:## D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:34:## D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:40:## D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:53:## D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:57:## D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:61:## D6. Errors never cross FFI as exceptions
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:73:## D7. Capabilities report; never decide policy
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:85:## D8. Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:87:The substrate that delivers D1, D2, D4, D5 in practice. Every inserted event participates in a composite reverse index that names the views interested in it; view recompute is bounded to ≤60 deltas per second per view; the working set is hot-resident with claim-pinned overlays. Allocations after warmup are linear in active-view count, never in cached-event count. False-wakeup rate ≤ 0.10 candidates per delta.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:89:The idle-tick emit path is gated on `kernel.changed_since_emit()` — idle ticks that produce no state change must not emit snapshots (D8 regression guard).

crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s5_reentrancy.rs:148:    //   - CB_IN_FLIGHT_TS_MS > 0   → callback entered at that epoch-ms; watchdog fires
crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s5_reentrancy.rs:149:    //                                 if (epoch.elapsed_ms - stored_ms) > WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS.
crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s5_reentrancy.rs:159:            let in_flight_ms = CB_IN_FLIGHT_TS_MS.load(Ordering::Acquire);
crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s5_reentrancy.rs:168:            if age_ms > WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS {
crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s5_reentrancy.rs:169:                WATCHDOG_FIRED.store(true, Ordering::Release);
crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s5_reentrancy.rs:172:                     (threshold {WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS} ms). Terminating."
crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s5_reentrancy.rs:207:    let watchdog_fired = WATCHDOG_FIRED.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s5_reentrancy.rs:220:    // G-S5 gates — per docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md §G-S5.

crates/nmp-testing/tests/m2_plan_id_stability.rs:12://! Doctrine: D8 (plan-id stability avoids redundant recompilation).
crates/nmp-testing/tests/m2_plan_id_stability.rs:12://! Doctrine: D8 (plan-id stability avoids redundant recompilation).
docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D8): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 negentropy first, REQ second · D3 outbox routing automatic, manual relay is the opt-out · D4 single writer per fact; caches derive · D5 snapshots bounded by open views · D6 errors never cross FFI as exceptions · D7 capabilities report, never decide policy · D8 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60 Hz/view, working-set bounded). Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
crates/nmp-core/src/store/mem/insert.rs:3://! D4: ONE writer. All event mutations flow through here.
crates/nmp-core/src/store/mem/insert.rs:4://! D2: Returns typed `InsertOutcome`; never panics.

docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:213:### D5: Capabilities Report, Never Decide — COMPLIANT

docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:42:**User picked option (b):** expand `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 to formally absorb the three additional load-bearing rules (errors-never-FFI / capabilities-report / reactivity-≤60Hz) as named doctrines D6, D7, D8.
docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:44:Product-spec now has D0–D8 with an explicit "two kinds" distinction:
docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:45:- **D0–D5: policy doctrines** — user-facing semantics (kernel-boundary, best-effort rendering, negentropy-first, outbox-automatic, single-writer-per-fact, snapshots-bounded).
docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:46:- **D6–D8: substrate invariants** — runtime / FFI / hot-path constraints (errors-never-FFI, capabilities-report, reactivity-contract).
docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:48:Conflicts still resolve in listed order (D0 wins over D8). README aligned. T19 framework-magic-reconciler in flight will absorb D0–D8 into the framework-magic docs (sending them an updated brief alongside this commit).

docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:81:The doctrines from `product-spec.md` §1.5 (D1 best-effort rendering, D2 negentropy first, D3 outbox automatic, D4 single writer per fact, D5 snapshots bounded by what's open) survive intact and apply across all modules.

docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:27:## 1.5 Cardinal doctrines D0–D8
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:Summary: D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines (user-facing semantics); D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* (runtime implementation constraints). Both are equally binding. Every API decision answers to at least one; conflicts resolve in the order D0 > D1 > … > D8.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:35:| D0 | No app nouns in `nmp-core`; test surface gated behind `test-support` feature | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:36:| D1 | Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:37:| D2 | Negentropy first, REQ second | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:38:| D3 | Outbox routing automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:39:| D4 | Single writer per fact; caches derive | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:40:| D5 | Snapshots bounded by what's open | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:41:| D6 | Errors never cross FFI as exceptions | Substrate |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:42:| D7 | Capabilities report; never decide policy | Substrate |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:43:| D8 | Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded; idle-tick emit gated on `changed_since_emit()` | Substrate |

docs/design/nip29/routing.md:18:1. **D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders** — the diagnostics lane (ADR-0007) wouldn't see the wire activity because it didn't go through the compiler.
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:3:> **Audience:** Framework contributors. Complements `reactivity-bench` (synthetic, Rust-only); this harness exercises the full system end-to-end.
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:15:**Modeled budget contract** — the harness's deterministic replay mode runs synthetic workloads through a *model* of the runtime: simulated relay sockets, simulated storage, simulated UniFFI marshaling, simulated NSE. Passing the gates here proves the budgets are internally consistent and the harness scaffolding works. It does **not** prove the real runtime hits those budgets.
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:25:`reactivity-bench` proved the algorithmic core scales: reverse-index lookup, view recompute, delta emission. It is synthetic — single Rust process, no relays, no disk, no real-world event distribution, no FFI.
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:264:| Delta volume / coalescing | ✓ (synthetic streams) | ✓ (real bursty traffic) |
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:330:After Phase 1 lands (event store + planner + reactivity machinery), build `live` and `capture` modes minimally, capture the first trace, and run `cold_start` + `relay_disconnect_storm` scenarios. Use the results to validate ADR-0001/0002/0003 against real network patterns (the synthetic harness can't show, e.g., what happens when 5 relays send the same event within 50ms of each other).
docs/decisions/0011-lmdb-env-sharing.md:17:Per doctrine D6: "errors never cross FFI as exceptions." The corollary is that the *store itself* must never silently produce an incorrect answer because of partial writes. Any scenario where two separate `lmdb::Environment` handles could each commit independently would violate this — we would have no way to roll back one side when the other fails.
docs/decisions/0011-lmdb-env-sharing.md:19:**Why this matters (D6):** if the store returns wrong data because of a partial write, that wrong data eventually reaches FFI as an `AppUpdate`. There is no way to signal "this snapshot is partially corrupt" through a `Result<T, StoreError>` once the data is already in the `AppUpdate` struct. The only safe design is to prevent partial writes at the store layer.
docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:232:- **Encode non-Nostr records as synthetic Nostr events.** Rejected. This pollutes event-store invariants and makes canonical filter/query behavior ambiguous.
docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:242:- A synthetic non-Nostr module test writes domain records, action-ledger rows, capability reports, and side effects, then verifies each crosses the correct bridge lane.

docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:94:**Exit gate.** Cold launch with primed LMDB renders the seed-driven timeline in ≤ 1.5s, showing fresh content from hundreds of authors followed by the seeds; tap an author → profile screen → back works; kind:0 arriving mid-scroll updates all author rows in place per doctrine D1; updating one seed's kind:3 mid-session re-resolves the timeline's author set without manual intervention; reactivity-bench `--standard` continues to pass at the larger author-set size; firehose-bench `live` for `sustained_firehose` (running at the real seed-author scale, not modeled) lands within budgets.
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D6, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.

docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:283:- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).

crates/nmp-core/src/store/types/errors.rs:3://! D6: store errors cross FFI only via `Result<_, StoreError>` translated at

crates/nmp-core/src/store/types/errors.rs:55:    /// D8 / GC ceiling invariant — see `docs/design/lmdb/gc.md` §2.
docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:60:This bullet is a load-bearing demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D7** ("capabilities report; never decide policy" — `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D7). The KeyringCapability **reports** (here is the stored bytes; persistence succeeded/failed). It does **not decide** (whether to retry, whether to fall back to a different storage backend, whether to surface a UI prompt). The framework decides; the capability executes.
docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:62:The test's assertion that no plaintext nsec crosses FFI is the structural witness for **D6** ("errors never cross FFI as exceptions"; `overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D6) and for **D5** (bounded native state: the platform layer never sees unencrypted key material because every read/write of the key goes through the in-Rust `IdentityModule::sign` function).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:176:  property is critical for D8 (reactivity contract — §1.5): recompilation work must be bounded
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:190:| `startup_requests` (requests.rs:50-106) | **Relocated outside `nmp-core` per D0.** The seed timeline / kind:0 / kind:10002 / kind:3 bootstrap is social graph knowledge — not kernel substrate. It moves to `nmp-nip01` / `nmp-nip02` / the demo app's startup sequence. `nmp-core` only executes interests registered by modules; it does not hard-code which interests those are. The TEST_PUBKEY-specific bootstrap (line 71-82) moves to the demo app (or a test fixture in `nmp-testing`). The kernel's `ActorStart` handler calls `compiler.recompile(Trigger::Startup)`, which is a no-op until a module registers interests. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:208:| `maybe_open_timeline` (ingest.rs:329-365) | The "seed-contacts arrive → open union timeline" logic moves to `nmp-nip02` (follows/contacts module), not to an `nmp-core` helper. D0: `nmp-core` must not know the social graph. The contacts module watches the kind:3 projection and registers a `Timeline { authors: union }` interest on behalf of whichever view asked for it. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:217:  into `nmp-core` — those are module concerns (D0). The impl PR for M2 must register those

crates/nmp-core/src/store/types/ids.rs:3://! D0: belongs to `nmp-core::store`; no cross-module dependency.
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:54:## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/design/nip29-crate.md:23:Treating NIP-29 as "just another kind range" would force NMP's kernel actor or M2 compiler to grow group-aware special cases, violating ADR-0009 doctrine D0 ("the kernel never grows app nouns"). The crate is the boundary where the special cases live; the kernel sees a generic `RelayPinnedInterest` and `RelayPinnedPublish` it already knows how to handle (see `routing.md`).
docs/design/nip29-crate.md:29:├── nmp-core/                  # kernel substrate — D0: knows nothing of groups
docs/design/framework-magic/kind3.md:22:**Failure mode prevented:** the canonical NDK-era bug: app code listens for kind:3 events, manually closes its open subscriptions, re-derives author lists, re-issues REQs, and either races itself (REQ ordering vs. local-state ordering) or leaks the old REQ. This contract structurally forbids that pattern: the view module never sees the kind:3 directly, and the app never issues a REQ. Specifically discharges **D3** (outbox routing automatic; see `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D3) and the auto-group/auto-close property of **C8** for the follow-list-change case.
docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:15:**Framework does:** the subscription-compilation pipeline (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md`) for dedup and coalesce; the wire-emitter's diff (compiler §3 final stage) for auto-close on plan changes; the view registry's refcount drop for auto-close on consumer loss; `docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md` for the per-tick batching; the FullState backpressure fallback at `subsystems.md` §7.2 line 69. The ≤60Hz cap is D8 (reactivity contract per `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D8; budget validated by ADR-0002 in `docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md` §7.2).
docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:34:The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. D5 (snapshots bounded by what's open) covers the view-scoped auto-close property; D8 (reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded) covers the ≤60Hz emit cap, per `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D8. Different milestone responsibility from C6/C7.
crates/nmp-core/src/store/types/mod.rs:16:pub use events::{RawEvent, VerifiedEvent, StoredEvent};
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:21:The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D8 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5). The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:25:| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:26:| **D1** best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | C13 (placeholders), C1–C4 (refinement triggers), C5 (kind:3 → re-render of follow-derived views) |
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:27:| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:28:| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:29:| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:30:| **D5** snapshots bounded by what's open | C8 (view-scoped subscriptions auto-close when views close; payload set bounded to open views) |
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:31:| **D6** errors never cross FFI as exceptions | C7 (publish fail → `PublishPlanError` state, not exception); C11 (signer errors → ledger state); implicitly all 13 via the actor's toast-surface design |
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:32:| **D7** capabilities report; never decide policy | C11 (KeyringCapability reports bytes, framework decides retry/activation) |
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:33:| **D8** reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded | C8 sub-path 4 (≤60Hz emit cap); C13 (in-place refinement via projection cache) |
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:57:1. **It forces honesty.** If a chapter cannot fill the "App writes" field with `"nothing"` or a single safe call, the framework has leaked the operation to the app, and the doctrine D0 boundary is violated. The author of that chapter is required to file an ADR rather than ship the bullet as-is.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:28:Four assertions corresponding to the four M2 exit-gate bullets in [`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md), plus one substrate-invariant assertion (Assertion 5) covering D8 address-pointer dedup added by T24:
docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:246:> "Two views registering the same `NaddrCoord` emit ONE REQ per relay (Rule 8 address-pointer union, D8 invariant)."
docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:272:> "Two views registering the same `NaddrCoord` emit ONE REQ per relay (Rule 8 address-pointer union, D8 invariant)."
docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:298:> "Two views registering the same `NaddrCoord` emit ONE REQ per relay (Rule 8 address-pointer union, D8 invariant)."
docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:324:> "Two views registering the same `NaddrCoord` emit ONE REQ per relay (Rule 8 address-pointer union, D8 invariant)."
docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:388:- **Real wire frames against a relay.** This is `firehose-bench live` per [`docs/plan/m1-twitter-slice.md`](../../plan/m1-twitter-slice.md) (M1 exit gate "Firehose-bench live cold_start"); the audit test is offline and synthetic.
crates/nmp-core/src/store/types/gc.rs:3://! D8: GC ceiling defaults (1000 per-view, 20000 global pinned) are documented
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:34:**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:78:/// Used in D8 substrate invariant: the composite reverse index extends to
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:161:This is the D8 substrate invariant applied to address pointers: the composite reverse index, when extended to `NaddrCoord`, deduplicates across views without any view-module coordination.
docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:4:> Read first: `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 doctrine D1; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 (the per-field placeholder table + the `TimelineItem` concrete example); `docs/aim.md` §4.12; `docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md`.
docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:22:**Failure mode prevented:** the entire class of "Nostr-client cold-start UI" bugs `subsystems.md` §1.5 D1 enumerates as ruled out by construction:
docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:29:The bug-extinction surface in `overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 does not have a single numbered bug for this because the failures are UX defects rather than data-corruption bugs, but the doctrine clause D1 is the explicit promise the contract holds.
docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:45:**Milestone owner:** **[PARTIAL]** for placeholder shape — the M1 timeline slice ships `author_display` as a non-`Option` `String` with shortened-npub fallback (verified in `crates/nmp-core` timeline tests). However, `author_picture_url` is currently `Option<String>` in the M1 payload: when no kind:0 is present the field is `None` rather than a deterministic identicon URI, which violates D1's "every display field carries a value" guarantee. The D1-compliant fix is to make `author_picture_url: String` non-optional and populate it with a deterministic identicon URI derived from the pubkey hash when no picture URL is known — making the placeholder computable without any network call and without an `Option` that tempts the platform to branch on `None`. This change lands in M2 alongside the view-module surface refactor. **[PENDING M2/M3]** for the full in-place refinement guarantees: sub-paths 2 and 4 require the kernel's projection cache (`kernel-substrate.md` §3 line 148 `on_projection_changed`) which graduates in M2; sub-path 3 requires the per-tick re-format hook (`fn on_tick`, M2's `ViewModule` trait work).
docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:55:C13 is the canonical instance of cardinal doctrine **D1**. The doctrine clause's wording — *"There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API"* — is testable through the payload shape itself, which is what sub-path 1's "no `loading` field" assertion checks. The framework cannot guarantee the app does not implement its own spinner over the payload, but it can guarantee the API does not give the app a way to ask the question that would justify one.
docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:57:C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.
docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:59:C13 is delivered in practice by **D8** (reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60Hz/view · working-set bounded). The projection cache's composite reverse index (`docs/design/reactivity/loop-and-reverse-index.md` ADR-0001) is what makes `on_projection_changed` fire only for the views that mention Alice, not for every view in the store.

crates/nmp-core/src/store/types/watermark.rs:3://! D3 (sync): watermarks are persisted across launches; Coverage drives relay
docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:41:C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.

docs/plan/m1-twitter-slice.md:37:The `profile_thrashing` and `mount_unmount_churn` scenarios note "process RSS ≤ baseline + N MB" as assertions, but M1 has no OS-level instrumentation to measure them (requires `mach_task_basic_info` on Apple, `/proc/<pid>/status` on Linux). M10.5 is the dedicated FFI-hardening + iPhone-12 measurement pass; it adds scenario S11 to capture RSS via `mach_task_basic_info` during the canonical workflow on real hardware. The numeric gate (≤ 100 MB simulator / ≤ 150 MB iPhone 12 at 100 active views per ADR-0003 working-set gate) lives in M10.5 S11 / gates.md §G-S11.
docs/design/podcast/podcast-feeds.md:122:All actions return parsed records. None of them write to the domain store directly — `podcast-core::SubscribePodcast`/`RefreshFeed` orchestrate the writes, preserving the doctrine: **one writer per fact** (D4).

docs/plan/status.md:10:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:15:You are reviewing merge f1e374b (perf(m1): clean-build verification baseline) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:16:- D0: kernel never grows app nouns (nmp-core stays substrate-only; per ADR-0009)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:17:- D1: best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place; no spinners gating renderable content
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:18:- D2: negentropy first, REQ second; every filter/relay pair is a tracked sync target
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:19:- D3: outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:20:- D4: single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:21:- D5: snapshots bounded by what's open; AppState carries view payloads only for open views

docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:110:**Doctrine review (D0–D8, policy doctrines D0–D5 + substrate invariants D6–D8):**
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:111:- D0 (kernel boundary): `ActorCommand` and `spawn_actor` are now gated behind
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:113:- D1 (best-effort rendering): unchanged — still one actor thread; no render path touched
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:114:- D2 (negentropy first): no subscription policy changed
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:115:- D3 (outbox automatic): no relay code touched
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:116:- D4 (single writer per fact): the poll only calls `pending_view_requests()`,
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:118:- D5 (snapshots bounded): no new view kinds added
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:119:- D6 (errors never cross FFI): no FFI surface changed
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:120:- D7 (capabilities report): no capability decisions added
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:121:- D8 (reactivity contract): `emit_now` is now gated on `changed_since_emit()`
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:131:**Doctrine review (D0–D8):**
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:132:- D0–D5: Same reasoning as above.  `maybe_open_timeline()` is idempotent; it
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:134:- D6–D8: No FFI, capability, or reactivity-budget changes.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:17:You are reviewing a session's worth of merges on master in the nostr-multi-platform repo. NMP is a Rust multiplatform framework for Nostr apps building toward v1 per docs/plan.md. Doctrine D0–D5 (docs/product-spec.md §1.5):
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:18:- D0 kernel never grows app nouns
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:19:- D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:20:- D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60 Hz/view, working-set bound)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:21:- D3 errors never cross FFI (become toast state fields)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:22:- D4 one writer per fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:23:- D5 capabilities report, never decide
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:47:- All 5 cardinal doctrines (D0–D5) compliant
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:57:✅ D0 (kernel never grows app nouns): kernel is domain-agnostic
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:58:✅ D1 (best-effort rendering): ProfileCard.placeholder renders immediately
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:59:✅ D2 (reactivity contract): all updates flow through composite reverse index
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:60:✅ D3 (errors never cross FFI): errors as advisory JSON data, not FFI codes
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:61:✅ D4 (one writer per fact): kernel actor is single-threaded
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:62:✅ D5 (capabilities report): iOS bridge is pure relay, no policy decisions
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:387:+### D0 Audit: Kernel Never Grows App Nouns

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:509:+- All cardinal doctrines (D0–D5) are compliant; no design changes needed.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:537:+**Code Quality:** Zero bugs. All 20 code inspection findings are acceptable or justified by design. All cardinal doctrines (D0–D5) are upheld.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:578:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:614:+  - Error-shape exhaustion: every typed FFI error path exercised; assert each one becomes a `toast: Option<String>` state field, never a thrown exception across the boundary (D3).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/7d16b3f.md:15:2. **`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:236`** — duplicate cache hits return before `VerifiedEvent::try_from_raw` and `store.insert`, so D4 is still broken for duplicate relay delivery. Provenance never merges in `EventStore`, and invalid duplicate payloads can still bump `relay_count`. Route duplicates through store first, then update the read-cache from the store outcome.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/7d16b3f.md:21:4. **`crates/nmp-core/src/store/types/events.rs:101`** and **`crates/nmp-core/src/store/mem/insert.rs:28`** — stale "verification deferred" comments remain even though `VerifiedEvent` is now wired. Clean them up.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/7d16b3f.md:27:Store invariants have good tests, but the production verification path is not covered: all store tests use `VerifiedEvent::from_raw_unchecked`. Add a real signed-event fixture for `try_from_raw`, plus regressions for malformed short IDs and duplicate relay provenance through kernel ingest.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/7d16b3f.md:31:No direct FFI files changed. `EventStore::insert(RawEvent -> VerifiedEvent)` change is Rust public API, not current FFI surface. No ADR required.
crates/nmp-core/src/planner/interest.rs:8://! Doctrine: D3 (outbox routing automatic), D6 (errors are internal Results),
crates/nmp-core/src/planner/interest.rs:9://!           D8 (composite reverse index, zero per-event allocs after warmup).
crates/nmp-core/src/planner/interest.rs:46:/// of the merge lattice) and by the D8 composite reverse index to deduplicate

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:475:docs/design/podcast/inventory.md:65:| `Utilities/ErrorPresentation.swift` | 58 | swift | `ios/NmpPodcast/Bridge/ErrorPresentation.swift` | UI-only — `AppError`/`ErrorHandler` map `toast: Option<String>` from `AppState` (doctrine D3). No business logic. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:482:docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:85:Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:484:docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:106:- The doctrine review at `docs/perf/m11/doctrine-review.md` signs off D0–D5 against the M11 surface (template: `docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:486:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:242:17 `ViewModule`s. Each in `podcast-core/src/views/<name>.rs`. All share `View*` payload conventions: pre-formatted strings (per doctrine D1), no business logic in Swift, composite-keyed `ViewDependencies` (per ADR-0001).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:500:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:4:> Substrate reference: [`../kernel-substrate.md`](../kernel-substrate.md) §5; doctrine: D5 (capabilities report, never decide).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:504:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:85:### Bounded-state proof (D5)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:509:docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:106:- "No business logic in native." (Doctrine D0 + AGENTS.md guardrails.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:516:docs/design/podcast/podcast-feeds.md:122:All actions return parsed records. None of them write to the domain store directly — `podcast-core::SubscribePodcast`/`RefreshFeed` orchestrate the writes, preserving the doctrine: **one writer per fact** (D4).

It says shortened npub parsed from an `a`-tag plus `"loading"-style content`, but D1's placeholder contract says content body placeholder is empty string with no loading gate. Also an `e`-tag target has no expected author. Split this into address-placeholder vs event-id-placeholder expectations and avoid "loading-style" language.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:165:+use std::sync::{Mutex, OnceLock};
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:170: const WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS: u64 = 5_000;
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:175:+/// 0 in CB_IN_FLIGHT_TS_MS means no callback is in flight.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:176:+static EPOCH: OnceLock<Instant> = OnceLock::new();
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:178:+/// Reset to 0 on callback return.  Watchdog: fires if non-zero AND age > WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:179:+static CB_IN_FLIGHT_TS_MS: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:186: static WATCHDOG_FIRED: AtomicBool = AtomicBool::new(false);
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:200:+    CB_IN_FLIGHT_TS_MS.store(t_entry_ms.max(1), Ordering::Release);
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:209:+            CB_IN_FLIGHT_TS_MS.store(0, Ordering::Release);
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:225:+    CB_IN_FLIGHT_TS_MS.store(0, Ordering::Release);
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:231:     WATCHDOG_FIRED.store(false, Ordering::Release);
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:237:+    // Initialise epoch; CB_IN_FLIGHT_TS_MS = 0 means no callback in flight.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:239:+    CB_IN_FLIGHT_TS_MS.store(0, Ordering::Release);
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:248:-    // If an emit callback has been running for > WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS without
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:253:+    //   - CB_IN_FLIGHT_TS_MS == 0  → no callback in flight (safe).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:254:+    //   - CB_IN_FLIGHT_TS_MS > 0   → callback entered at that epoch-ms; watchdog fires
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:255:+    //                                 if (epoch.elapsed_ms - stored_ms) > WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:270:-                if gap > WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:271:-                    WATCHDOG_FIRED.store(true, Ordering::Release);
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:274:-                         (threshold {WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS} ms). Terminating."
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:278:+            let in_flight_ms = CB_IN_FLIGHT_TS_MS.load(Ordering::Acquire);
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:287:+            if age_ms > WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:288:+                WATCHDOG_FIRED.store(true, Ordering::Release);
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:291:+                     (threshold {WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS} ms). Terminating."

docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:301:-            .with_note("G-S5: zero deadlocks (5 s external watchdog)"),
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:302:+            .with_note("G-S5: zero deadlocks (5 s external watchdog; epoch-absolute timestamps)"),

docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:15:You are reviewing merge 55dd5f2 on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:16:- D0: kernel never grows app nouns (nmp-core stays substrate-only; per ADR-0009)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:17:- D1: best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place; no spinners gating renderable content
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:18:- D2: negentropy first, REQ second; every filter/relay pair is a tracked sync target
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:19:- D3: outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:20:- D4: single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:21:- D5: snapshots bounded by what's open; AppState carries view payloads only for open views
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:48:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:1289:- `docs/plan.md:9` misstates the cardinal doctrine. It replaces canonical D2/D3/D5 with “reactivity contract,” “errors never cross FFI,” and “capabilities report.” Fix to match `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:54-79`: D2 negentropy first, D3 automatic outbox routing, D5 snapshots bounded by open views. Also relabel `docs/plan.md:356`; FFI error shape is not D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:1295:- `docs/plan.md:437` says `LocalNotificationCapability` is extended for “episode-available alerts” inside the reusable kernel capability set. That weakens D0. Make the capability generic; keep episode semantics in `podcast-core`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:1306:- `docs/plan.md:9` misstates the cardinal doctrine. It replaces canonical D2/D3/D5 with “reactivity contract,” “errors never cross FFI,” and “capabilities report.” Fix to match `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:54-79`: D2 negentropy first, D3 automatic outbox routing, D5 snapshots bounded by open views. Also relabel `docs/plan.md:356`; FFI error shape is not D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:1312:- `docs/plan.md:437` says `LocalNotificationCapability` is extended for “episode-available alerts” inside the reusable kernel capability set. That weakens D0. Make the capability generic; keep episode semantics in `podcast-core`.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/59f8d1b.md:862:2. `docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:64,265-269` still leaves local model ownership ambiguous: model exists both on `LlmRoute::RigProvider.model` and `RigProvider::Local { ..., model }`, while `NMP_LLM_MODEL` does not say which one it populates. That is a D4 single-writer risk.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/59f8d1b.md:871:2. `docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:64,265-269` still leaves local model ownership ambiguous: model exists both on `LlmRoute::RigProvider.model` and `RigProvider::Local { ..., model }`, while `NMP_LLM_MODEL` does not say which one it populates. That is a D4 single-writer risk.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:394:    27	## 1.5 Cardinal doctrines D0–D8
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:398:    31	Summary: D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines (user-facing semantics); D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* (runtime implementation constraints). Both are equally binding. Every API decision answers to at least one; conflicts resolve in the order D0 > D1 > … > D8.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:403:    36	| D1 | Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | Policy |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:404:    37	| D2 | Negentropy first, REQ second | Policy |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:405:    38	| D3 | Outbox routing automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out | Policy |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:406:    39	| D4 | Single writer per fact; caches derive | Policy |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:407:    40	| D5 | Snapshots bounded by what's open | Policy |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:408:    41	| D6 | Errors never cross FFI as exceptions | Substrate |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:409:    42	| D7 | Capabilities report; never decide policy | Substrate |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:722:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:35:| D0 | No app nouns in `nmp-core`; test surface gated behind `test-support` feature | Policy |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:779:     1	# Cardinal Doctrines D0–D8
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:785:     7	**Two kinds of doctrine.** D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines — they govern user-facing semantics (what the framework promises, what it forbids). D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* — they govern how the runtime is allowed to be implemented (what crosses FFI, how state changes propagate, what the hot path can do). Both kinds are equally binding; their distinction is the kind of review they enforce. Policy review flags "this API choice violates a user-facing principle"; substrate review flags "this implementation choice will leak across FFI / hide policy on the native side / degrade reactivity."
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:787:     9	## D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:799:    21	## D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:812:    34	## D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:818:    40	## D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:831:    53	## D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:835:    57	## D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:839:    61	## D6. Errors never cross FFI as exceptions

docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:871:1890:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:14:| 2026-05-18 03:45 | 6 | Heartbeat #6 (autonomous). MAJOR: T1 m1-hardener landed 701d0e5 — M1 PASSES empirically. Live firehose-bench cold_start 258ms first-item / 3587ms filled-timeline; profile_thrashing 0 leaked subs over 10min. M1 ✅ DONE. Two deferrals named (memory RSS → M10.5, dispatch-rate platform gate → M14) tracked as T22. T21 meta-subscribe-analyst landed b0ae439 (substrate-fit analysis: build as reference view module, ~30 LOC compiler change for address-pointer support); two own follow-up commits 5b86de4 + 6782b97 codex-cleaned. T13 m2-codex-fixer landed 5ff0ac5 (M2 design fixes round 1). User landed PD-001 RESOLVED option (b): expanded canonical doctrine D0-D5 → D0-D8 in product-spec/overview-and-dx.md §1.5 (added D6 errors-never-FFI / D7 capabilities-report / D8 reactivity-contract; two-kind classification: D0-D5 policy / D6-D8 substrate). README aligned in ea3d40e + aef42c9. Codex review of cumulative HB5 wave (b0ae439.md) flagged 5 issues — biggest is D0 violation: public ActorCommand exposes domain nouns (OpenAuthor/ClaimProfile/etc.) from nmp-core. Tracked as T23. Worktree cleanups: T1 (ad49d31a), T21 (a5a7f37a), T13 (a67512cc) removed. T22 + T23 dispatched this tick. Pending: none. 5 agents in flight: T14 m3-codex-fixer, T18 highlighter-explorer, T19 framework-magic-reconciler, T22 m1-deferral-folder, T23 m1-codex-fixer. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:872:1892:./README.md:29:- **Cardinal doctrine (D0–D8).** Canonical wording from [`docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5](docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md). Every PR is reviewed against this rubric. A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected. Conflicts resolve in the order listed. **Two kinds:** D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines (user-facing semantics); D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* (runtime / FFI / hot-path constraints). Both equally binding.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:873:1895:docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:7:**Two kinds of doctrine.** D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines — they govern user-facing semantics (what the framework promises, what it forbids). D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* — they govern how the runtime is allowed to be implemented (what crosses FFI, how state changes propagate, what the hot path can do). Both kinds are equally binding; their distinction is the kind of review they enforce. Policy review flags "this API choice violates a user-facing principle"; substrate review flags "this implementation choice will leak across FFI / hide policy on the native side / degrade reactivity."

docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:878:1956:docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:110:**Doctrine review (D0–D8, policy doctrines D0–D5 + substrate invariants D6–D8):**
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:879:1964:./docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D8): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 negentropy first, REQ second · D3 outbox routing automatic, manual relay is the opt-out · D4 single writer per fact; caches derive · D5 snapshots bounded by open views · D6 errors never cross FFI as exceptions · D7 capabilities report, never decide policy · D8 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60 Hz/view, working-set bounded). Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

T21 meta-subscribe-analyst landed b0ae439 (substrate-fit analysis: build as reference view module, ~30 LOC compiler change for address-pointer support); two own follow-up commits 5b86de4 + 6782b97 codex-cleaned. T13 m2-codex-fixer landed 5ff0ac5 (M2 design fixes round 1). User landed PD-001 RESOLVED option (b): expanded canonical doctrine D0-D5 → D0-D8 in product-spec/overview-and-dx.md §1.5 (added D6 errors-never-FFI / D7 capabilities-report / D8 reactivity-contract; two-kind classification: D0-D5 policy / D6-D8 substrate). README aligned in ea3d40e + aef42c9. Codex review of cumulative HB5 wave (b0ae439.md) flagged 5 issues — biggest is D0 violation: public ActorCommand exposes domain nouns (OpenAuthor/ClaimProfile/etc.) from nmp-core. Tracked as T23. Worktree cleanups: T1 (ad49d31a), T21 (a5a7f37a), T13 (a67512cc) removed. T22 + T23 dispatched this tick. Pending: none. 5 agents in flight: T14 m3-codex-fixer, T18 highlighter-explorer, T19 framework-magic-reconciler, T22 m1-deferral-folder, T23 m1-codex-fixer. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:2221: This bullet is a load-bearing demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D7** ("capabilities report; never decide policy" — `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D7). The KeyringCapability **reports** (here is the stored bytes; persistence succeeded/failed). It does **not decide** (whether to retry, whether to fall back to a different storage backend, whether to surface a UI prompt). The framework decides; the capability executes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:2223:-The test's assertion that no plaintext nsec crosses FFI is a key-security invariant: the platform layer never sees unencrypted key material because every signing operation goes through the in-Rust `IdentityModule::sign` function. Signer failures surface as `toast: Option<String>` state (per **D6**, "errors never cross FFI as exceptions"; `overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D6) rather than as exceptions or `Result<T, E>` across the FFI boundary.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:2224:+The test's assertion that no plaintext nsec crosses FFI is the structural witness for **D6** ("errors never cross FFI as exceptions"; `overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D6) and for **D5** (bounded native state: the platform layer never sees unencrypted key material because every read/write of the key goes through the in-Rust `IdentityModule::sign` function).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:2238:+> "Two views registering the same `NaddrCoord` emit ONE REQ per relay (Rule 8 address-pointer union, D8 invariant)."
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:2270: | 2026-05-18 07:07 | 18 | Heartbeat #18 (autonomous). Both T29 + T30 heavily active per worktree filesystem activity: T29 has split mem.rs → mem/ + types.rs → types/, modified kernel/ingest.rs + mod.rs + nostr.rs, ran firehose-bench live 3 times (M1 regression check). T30 has modified all 5 planner files + audit test. T31 STILL HELD — it needs T29's VerifiedEvent + EventStore::insert path for the S3/S4/S5 event-pressure injection. Once T29 lands, T31 can use the real API instead of waiting for relay. No completions this tick. Disk 95%, no master build issues. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:2271: | 2026-05-18 07:22 | 19 | Heartbeat #19 (autonomous). DOUBLE LANDING: T29 m3-followup-1 (7d16b3f) + T30 m2-followup-1 (bc84cfe) both pushed CLEAN via HEAD:master refspec (7th+8th agents on protocol). T29: mem.rs split (1067→357 LOC max), types.rs split, D4 ingest wired (with M1 firehose-bench live PASS evidence), VerifiedEvent + nostr crate added, GC ceilings (per-view 1000, global 20000), kind:5 max-merge. T30: Rule 1 wildcard correctness bug FIXED + test, plan-id hashes only referenced pubkeys + scope + version inputs (3 stability tests), RoutingSource::Indexer removed (→ UserConfigured/Indexer), direction table extended for #p/active-account-read/IndexerProbe, audit assertion 300→1000, mod.rs minimal exports, lattice.rs split per-rule, TODO removed. Codex on both flagged 8 round-2 issues (5 T29 + 3 T30): T29 P1 ingest pre-insert-return drops provenance, P1 only kinds 1|6 go through store (kinds 0/3/10002 still bypass), P2 GC intra-call BTreeSet missing, P2 release() leaves claim_budgets, P3 ID-prefix panic; T30 P1 partition.rs Case A returns before Case C (loses #p inbox routing), P2 inbox uses only read_relays not ∪ both_relays, P2 #p indexer fallback breaks structural ban → T32 + T33 filed. T31 m105-followup-1 dispatched now that T29 brought VerifiedEvent (S3/S4/S5 event-pressure injection unblocked). 3 agents in flight: T31, T32 + T33 pending. Disk 96%. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:5744:I’ve found one clear T31 mismatch already: the stress injection does not go through `VerifiedEvent` or `EventStore::insert`; it writes directly to the kernel read cache. I’m checking whether the remaining T31 gates are real assertions or only proxy/informational gates before I give the final verdict.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:5799:    27	## 1.5 Cardinal doctrines D0–D8
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:5803:    31	Summary: D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines (user-facing semantics); D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* (runtime implementation constraints). Both are equally binding. Every API decision answers to at least one; conflicts resolve in the order D0 > D1 > … > D8.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:5807:    35	| D0 | No app nouns in `nmp-core`; test surface gated behind `test-support` feature | Policy |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:5808:    36	| D1 | Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | Policy |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:5809:    37	| D2 | Negentropy first, REQ second | Policy |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:5810:    38	| D3 | Outbox routing automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out | Policy |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:5811:    39	| D4 | Single writer per fact; caches derive | Policy |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:5812:    40	| D5 | Snapshots bounded by what's open | Policy |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:5813:    41	| D6 | Errors never cross FFI as exceptions | Substrate |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:5814:    42	| D7 | Capabilities report; never decide policy | Substrate |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:5815:    43	| D8 | Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded; idle-tick emit gated on `changed_since_emit()` | Substrate |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:5987:    91	        --require-doctrines D0,D1,D2,D3,D4,D5 \

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:6654:db2f0ae docs(framework-magic): align with canonical D0-D5 + fix overclaims + replace TBD markers
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:6665:- `8660961` `crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s3_snapshot_pressure.rs:60`, `s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs:63`, `s5_reentrancy.rs:95`: S3/S4/S5 inject synthetic events into the read cache, not `VerifiedEvent` + `EventStore::insert`. `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:427` writes directly to `self.events`. Fix: create signed/valid events and route through the normal verify/store ingest path.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:6666:- `8660961` `crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s1_mount_unmount.rs:117`, `s2_dispatch_flood.rs:154`, `s3_snapshot_pressure.rs:72`, `s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs:160`: gates are not enforced per `gates.md`. Examples: S1 uses 60% not 90%; S2 uses 98% not 600k/100%; S3 uses 1k not 100k; S4 stale-rev/apply gates are missing or hardcoded. Fix: gate exact spec metrics or fail as unobservable.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:6683:- `8660961` `crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s3_snapshot_pressure.rs:60`, `s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs:63`, `s5_reentrancy.rs:95`: S3/S4/S5 inject synthetic events into the read cache, not `VerifiedEvent` + `EventStore::insert`. `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:427` writes directly to `self.events`. Fix: create signed/valid events and route through the normal verify/store ingest path.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:6684:- `8660961` `crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s1_mount_unmount.rs:117`, `s2_dispatch_flood.rs:154`, `s3_snapshot_pressure.rs:72`, `s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs:160`: gates are not enforced per `gates.md`. Examples: S1 uses 60% not 90%; S2 uses 98% not 600k/100%; S3 uses 1k not 100k; S4 stale-rev/apply gates are missing or hardcoded. Fix: gate exact spec metrics or fail as unobservable.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:218:./docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:15:You are reviewing merge f68a479 (docs(m10.5): fix codex re-review residuals — D1/D5 summary table, trailing whitespace) on master in nostr-multi-platform. This commit applies minor residual fixes from a codex re-review of fb139ab. Doctrine D0-D5:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:16:- D0: kernel never grows app nouns
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:17:- D1: best-effort rendering
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:18:- D2: negentropy first
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:19:- D3: outbox routing automatic
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:20:- D4: single writer per fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:21:- D5: snapshots bounded by what is open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:32:f68a479 docs(m10.5): fix codex re-review residuals (D1/D5 summary table, trailing whitespace)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:39:@@ -245,11 +245,11 @@ Full D0–D5 line-item-to-scenario mapping in
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:42: | **D0** kernel never grows app nouns | debt-inventory §3 D0 audit + S6 (the kernel does not grow capability variants under churn) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:43:-| **D1** best-effort rendering with placeholders | S3 (snapshot pressure) + S10 (long suspend) — placeholder-then-refine path |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:44:+| **D1** best-effort rendering with placeholders | S3 (snapshot pressure) + S4 (reconciler back-pressure) + code proof (ProfileCard.placeholder) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:45: | **D2** ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound | S2, S3, S8 — emit-rate cap, planner dedup |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:46: | **D3** errors never cross FFI | S7 (exhaustion) + §7.2 (toast bridge) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:47: | **D4** one writer per fact | S1, S5 — refcount only mutated on actor thread; reentrancy under same single-writer rule |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:48:-| **D5** snapshots bounded by what's open | S1 (refcount drives eviction) + S3 (full-state size scales with open views, not store) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:49:+| **D5** capabilities report, never decide | CapabilityModule code proof + S6 (relay capability lifecycle storms) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:137:Verify: the prior residuals (D1/D5 table, whitespace) are fixed, no new issues introduced. Be terse.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:141:/bin/zsh -lc "rg -n \"D0|D1|D2|D3|D4|D5|capabilities report|snapshots bounded|best-effort|outbox routing|single writer\" docs/design/ffi-hardening.md docs/perf/m10.5 docs -g '*.md'" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a8c0acab50f455ada
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:146:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:19:   (`docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D0–D5), and every ownership
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:147:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:66:| 8 | `nmp_app_open_author(*mut, *const c_char)` | `void nmp_app_open_author(void *app, const char *pubkey)` | UTF-8 C string, expected 64-char lowercase hex pubkey. Hex-validated via `is_hex_pubkey`. Trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace. Empty / non-hex inputs are **silently dropped** (see §7 finding D3-gap). | Silent no-op on null app or null pubkey. |

Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:179:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:10:| 2026-05-18 02:10 | 2 | Heartbeat #2. Landed: T9 ffi-safety-commenter 03d7a23 (SAFETY comments accurate, semantics unchanged, cargo+tests pass; codex flagged pre-existing clippy lints in actor.rs+reactivity-bench → T12); m1-hardener live.rs prototype stashed (was polluting main tree again). Two outstanding codex reviews caught up: codex on 031fc07 found 8 substantive M2-design issues (no-publish-to-indexer, D4 single-writer, D0 module ownership, plan-id churn, lane modeling) → T13; codex on 9fead0e found 10 substantive M3-design issues (atomicity ADR blocker, D2 not enforced through trait, GC pinned-overlay unbounded, kind:5 a-tag deletes missing, tombstone semantics, key encoding mismatches, scan_by_kind_dtag missing from trait) → T14. Worktree agent-a41054fd04781ce06 cleaned. T10 (plan.md split) dispatched. 4 agents in flight: m1-hardener, codex-fixer-1, codex-fixer-2, plan-splitter. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:185:docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:167:## Doctrine Audits D0–D5

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:192:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:193:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:194:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:195:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:11:### PD-001 — Doctrine vocabulary collision: D0–D5 vs RMP-bible invariants vs reactivity ADRs
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:196:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:13:**Decision (autonomous):** The canonical D0–D5 is what's in `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 (kernel-boundary, best-effort, negentropy-first, outbox-automatic, single-writer-per-fact, snapshots-bounded). I corrected the README to match (commit pending in next push).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:197:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:15:**Why:** Codex review of merge `51120cb` flagged that I had been using a different D2/D3/D5 mapping throughout this session — conflating the product-spec D0–D5 with RMP-bible invariants (errors-never-cross-FFI is invariant #2; capabilities-report-don't-decide is RMP cardinal rule #6; ≤60 Hz reactivity is from ADR-0001..0004). They're all real binding rules, but they're three different rule sources. I had collapsed them into a single "D0–D5" rubric, which was wrong.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:198:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:19:- Various codex review prompts I wrote earlier passed the wrong D0–D5 to codex. The flagged findings in those reviews are still valid (codex review the doctrine word-by-word), but the framing was off.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:199:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:20:- `docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md` — uses the canonical D0–D5 correctly (codex flagged + fixed earlier).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:200:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:22:**Open question for user:** Do you want me to (a) keep the canonical product-spec D0–D5 as the single rubric and downgrade the RMP-bible invariants + reactivity-ADR rules to "supporting rules" (cited by number where relevant); or (b) expand the canonical D0–D5 to incorporate the three additional load-bearing rules (would require editing `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 — that's the authoritative source so changing it is a meaningful commit).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:201:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:24:**Current default while you decide:** option (a). README + framework-magic docs will be aligned to product-spec D0–D5; supporting rules cited by their source (RMP-bible invariant N, ADR-NNNN, etc.).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:202:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:203:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:41:### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:204:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:54:### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:205:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:206:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:73:### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:207:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:77:### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:210:docs/design/view-catalog/stubs-validation-next.md:56:- **Computed-from-native fields.** All formatting, all derivations live in Rust per doctrine D5.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:211:docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:103:- **Bare pubkey path.** If no kind:0 exists in the store yet, `open()` returns a placeholder-filled payload and the reverse-index dependency triggers `on_event_inserted` when one arrives. Doctrine D1 prohibits returning `None` here.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:212:docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:234:- **Author kind:0 not yet loaded.** Per doctrine D1, render the item with placeholders. The `by_author` reindex on `on_projection_changed` updates the item in place. **The post is rendered immediately.**
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:213:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:242:17 `ViewModule`s. Each in `podcast-core/src/views/<name>.rs`. All share `View*` payload conventions: pre-formatted strings (per doctrine D1), no business logic in Swift, composite-keyed `ViewDependencies` (per ADR-0001).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:214:docs/perf/m1/build-verification.md:139:- Simulator chosen: **iPhone 17 / iOS 26.5** (UUID `C380BA01-AD22-4F4A-8524-A309DA15144C`)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:215:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:280:- Failure-mode test: capability emits `Error { reason: "rate_limit" }` mid-stream → `ChatTurn` commits with `is_error: true` and the `toast` field is set per doctrine D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:216:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:218:docs/design/podcast/podcast-feeds.md:122:All actions return parsed records. None of them write to the domain store directly — `podcast-core::SubscribePodcast`/`RefreshFeed` orchestrate the writes, preserving the doctrine: **one writer per fact** (D4).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:219:docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:85:Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:220:docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:106:- The doctrine review at `docs/perf/m11/doctrine-review.md` signs off D0–D5 against the M11 surface (template: `docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md`).

Hex-validated via `is_hex_pubkey`. Trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace. Empty / non-hex inputs are **silently dropped** (see §7 finding D3-gap). | Silent no-op on null app or null pubkey. |
