docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:288:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:578:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:379:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1800:+You are reviewing a session's worth of merges on master in the nostr-multi-platform repo. NMP is a Rust multiplatform framework for Nostr apps building toward v1 per docs/plan.md. Doctrine D0–D5 (docs/product-spec.md §1.5):
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:380:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1801:+- D0 kernel never grows app nouns
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:381:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1802:+- D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:382:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1803:+- D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60 Hz/view, working-set bound)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:383:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1804:+- D3 errors never cross FFI (become toast state fields)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:384:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1805:+- D4 one writer per fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:385:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1806:+- D5 capabilities report, never decide
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:386:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1830:+- All 5 cardinal doctrines (D0–D5) compliant
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:387:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1840:+✅ D0 (kernel never grows app nouns): kernel is domain-agnostic
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:388:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1841:+✅ D1 (best-effort rendering): ProfileCard.placeholder renders immediately
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:389:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1842:+✅ D2 (reactivity contract): all updates flow through composite reverse index
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:390:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1843:+✅ D3 (errors never cross FFI): errors as advisory JSON data, not FFI codes
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:391:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1844:+✅ D4 (one writer per fact): kernel actor is single-threaded
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:392:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1845:+✅ D5 (capabilities report): iOS bridge is pure relay, no policy decisions

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:403:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2361:++> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:473:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3913:docs/plan.md:20:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:474:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3914:docs/plan.md:65:4. **The doctrine rubric is final.** Every PR is reviewed against the cardinal doctrines (`product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D5). A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:545:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3990:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:546:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3991:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:547:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3992:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:548:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4005:     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:582:docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:613:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1419:docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:396:The FFI and iOS bridge code is **clean of blocking debt markers** (TODO/FIXME/unimplemented/panic). All 20 code inspection findings are either justified by design or actionable as documentation cleanup. All cardinal doctrines (D0–D5) are upheld.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:614:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1426:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:615:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1428:docs/plan.md:20:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:624:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1449:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:547:+- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:625:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1450:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1112:+- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:648:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1490:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2561:   215	- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:649:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1491:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3465:    15	- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:650:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1492:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3656:    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:651:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1498:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4387:- [nip65.md:192](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:192) plus [nip65.md:197](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:197): `nmp-nip65` depends on `nmp-core`, while `nmp-core::kernel::planner` consumes `nmp-nip65`. That is a Rust crate cycle and blurs D0. Fix by moving mailbox traits/types into `nmp-core` or a tiny no-core crate, with `nmp-nip65` only registering module behavior.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:652:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1499:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4389:- [nip65.md:6](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:6), [nip65.md:98](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:98), [compiler.md:52](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:52): mailbox facts have too many apparent writers/read models: `ingest_relay_list`, `MailboxCache`, and `MailboxesView::on_event_inserted`. D4 wants one writer per fact. Fix by making one canonical mailbox projection/cache writer; views only read that projection.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:653:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1500:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4397:- [compiler.md:166](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:166) and [compiler.md:184](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:184): startup timeline/profile/contact behavior remains framed as `nmp-core` migration work. D0 fix: move social bootstrap modules to `nmp-nip01` / `nmp-nip02` / demo app code; core should only execute registered module interests.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:654:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1502:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4410:- [nip65.md:192](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:192) plus [nip65.md:197](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:197): `nmp-nip65` depends on `nmp-core`, while `nmp-core::kernel::planner` consumes `nmp-nip65`. That is a Rust crate cycle and blurs D0. Fix by moving mailbox traits/types into `nmp-core` or a tiny no-core crate, with `nmp-nip65` only registering module behavior.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:655:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1503:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4412:- [nip65.md:6](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:6), [nip65.md:98](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:98), [compiler.md:52](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:52): mailbox facts have too many apparent writers/read models: `ingest_relay_list`, `MailboxCache`, and `MailboxesView::on_event_inserted`. D4 wants one writer per fact. Fix by making one canonical mailbox projection/cache writer; views only read that projection.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:657:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1507:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:81:The doctrines from `product-spec.md` §1.5 (D1 best-effort rendering, D2 negentropy first, D3 outbox automatic, D4 single writer per fact, D5 snapshots bounded by what's open) survive intact and apply across all modules.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:658:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1512:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:15:You are reviewing merge fcf8b431b8d86f1801fef2fad26e81fbf56506f4 on master in the nostr-multi-platform repo. Doctrine D0–D5 (kernel never grows app nouns; best-effort rendering; reactivity contract ≤60 Hz/view; errors never cross FFI; one writer per fact; capabilities report don't decide). File-size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:733:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1606:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2361:++> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:800:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1750:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3912:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:801:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1751:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3913:docs/plan.md:20:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:802:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1752:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3914:docs/plan.md:65:4. **The doctrine rubric is final.** Every PR is reviewed against the cardinal doctrines (`product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D5). A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:869:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1820:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3984:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:54:### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:871:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1822:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3986:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:73:### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:872:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1823:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3987:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:77:### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:873:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1824:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3990:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:874:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1825:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3991:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:875:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1826:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3992:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:876:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1827:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4005:     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:902:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1890:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:15:You are reviewing merge 9fead0e (M3 LMDB schema + EventStore trait + GC policy design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5. File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:903:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1912:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:126:+**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:904:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1942:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1159:+## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:906:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1951:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1388:Review: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 + D2 — composite reverse index + working-set bound must remain enforced through the trait); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance; (4) consistency of EventStore trait surface across the 6 sub-docs; (5) the choice of nostr-lmdb as on-disk byte store vs roll-our-own — is the gap analysis honest?; (6) the GC eviction algorithm + budget; (7) the migration plumbing's testability; (8) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:927:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1999:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3720:    85	### Bounded-state proof (D5)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:928:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2001:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3978:3. [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:79): `AudioPlaybackBridge` decides repeated `Load` is a no-op based on `currentEpisode`. That is playback policy in native, and it conflicts with the same doc’s D5 claim at [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:87) plus the lesson at [lessons.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:58). Fix: Rust decides whether to send `Load`; the bridge only executes requests and reports state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:929:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2002:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3980:4. [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:130), [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:231), and [podcast-rag.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:132): `podcast-core` stores `embedding_id`, says it is re-exported from `podcast-rag`, while `podcast-rag` depends on `podcast-core` and backfills the core record. That creates either a crate cycle or a D4 one-writer violation. Fix: define `EmbeddingId` in `podcast-core` or a shared types crate, and have core own any writes to core records after `podcast-rag` returns `Indexed`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:930:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2003:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3997:3. [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:79): `AudioPlaybackBridge` decides repeated `Load` is a no-op based on `currentEpisode`. That is playback policy in native, and it conflicts with the same doc’s D5 claim at [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:87) plus the lesson at [lessons.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:58). Fix: Rust decides whether to send `Load`; the bridge only executes requests and reports state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:931:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2004:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3999:4. [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:130), [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:231), and [podcast-rag.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:132): `podcast-core` stores `embedding_id`, says it is re-exported from `podcast-rag`, while `podcast-rag` depends on `podcast-core` and backfills the core record. That creates either a crate cycle or a D4 one-writer violation. Fix: define `EmbeddingId` in `podcast-core` or a shared types crate, and have core own any writes to core records after `podcast-rag` returns `Indexed`.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:935:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2029:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:936:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2030:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:937:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2031:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:962:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2070:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:576:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:971:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2104:docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:215:- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:972:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2107:docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:973:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2108:docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:85:Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1029:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2222:docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1040:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2253:2304:- `xcodegen generate` is required here after adding new Swift files, and the working device path was `xcodebuild -project WeightTracker.xcodeproj -scheme WeightTracker -destination 'platform=iOS,id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' build` followed by `xcrun devicectl device install app` and `xcrun devicectl device process launch` [Task 4][Task 5]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1041:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2254:2468:- the working iPhone path was `cargo build --target aarch64-apple-ios --release -p tenex-core`, then `xcodebuild -workspace TenexMVP.xcworkspace -scheme TenexMVP -configuration Debug -destination 'platform=iOS,id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' -derivedDataPath .codex-derived-ios-device -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .codex-derived-ios-device/SourcePackages build`, followed by `xcrun devicectl device install app` and `xcrun devicectl device process launch --terminate-existing com.tenex.mvp` [Task 1]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1042:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2255:2637:- the working iPhone deployment sequence was `xcodebuild ... -destination 'id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' -derivedDataPath /tmp/WinTheDayDerived build`, then `xcrun devicectl device install app`, then `xcrun devicectl device process launch`; `No provider was found` warnings from `devicectl` were noisy but non-fatal here [Task 3]

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1049:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2398:   181	**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1050:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2459:   242	Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1051:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2622:    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1052:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2679:     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1053:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2690:    20	- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1065:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:3417:   288	- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1067:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:4392:- High: [trait.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:235) exposes only raw `claim(claimer, ids)`, while [gc.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/lmdb/gc.md:13) relies on bounded `open_view_cover` from the composite reverse index. That does not enforce D2 through the trait. Fix: add claim budget/cover validation or a view-cover registration API that rejects/truncates over-broad covers and tests it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1068:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:4421:- High: [trait.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:235) exposes only raw `claim(claimer, ids)`, while [gc.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/lmdb/gc.md:13) relies on bounded `open_view_cover` from the composite reverse index. docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1070:docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:94:**Exit gate.** Cold launch with primed LMDB renders the seed-driven timeline in ≤ 1.5s, showing fresh content from hundreds of authors followed by the seeds; tap an author → profile screen → back works; kind:0 arriving mid-scroll updates all author rows in place per doctrine D1; updating one seed's kind:3 mid-session re-resolves the timeline's author set without manual intervention; reactivity-bench `--standard` continues to pass at the larger author-set size; firehose-bench `live` for `sustained_firehose` (running at the real seed-author scale, not modeled) lands within budgets.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3307:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:19:   (`docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D0–D5), and every ownership
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3308:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:66:| 8 | `nmp_app_open_author(*mut, *const c_char)` | `void nmp_app_open_author(void *app, const char *pubkey)` | UTF-8 C string, expected 64-char lowercase hex pubkey. Hex-validated via `is_hex_pubkey`. Trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace. Empty / non-hex inputs are **silently dropped** (see §7 finding D3-gap). | Silent no-op on null app or null pubkey. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3310:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:114:| S1 | Mount/unmount churn | actor recv + refcount | D5 (snapshot bounded), bible #5 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3311:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:116:| S3 | Snapshot pressure | listener serialization | bible #9 (≤60 Hz), D5 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3312:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:117:| S4 | Reconciler back-pressure | listener channel growth | bible #9, D1 |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3334:docs/design/view-catalog/stubs-validation-next.md:56:- **Computed-from-native fields.** All formatting, all derivations live in Rust per doctrine D5.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3339:docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3345:docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:103:- **Bare pubkey path.** If no kind:0 exists in the store yet, `open()` returns a placeholder-filled payload and the reverse-index dependency triggers `on_event_inserted` when one arrives. Doctrine D1 prohibits returning `None` here.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3346:docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:234:- **Author kind:0 not yet loaded.** Per doctrine D1, render the item with placeholders. The `by_author` reindex on `on_projection_changed` updates the item in place. **The post is rendered immediately.**
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3358:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:282:## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3359:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:288:- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3362:docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.

Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

Two outstanding codex reviews caught up: codex on 031fc07 found 8 substantive M2-design issues (no-publish-to-indexer, D4 single-writer, D0 module ownership, plan-id churn, lane modeling) → T13; codex on 9fead0e found 10 substantive M3-design issues (atomicity ADR blocker, D2 not enforced through trait, GC pinned-overlay unbounded, kind:5 a-tag deletes missing, tombstone semantics, key encoding mismatches, scan_by_kind_dtag missing from trait) → T14. Worktree agent-a41054fd04781ce06 cleaned. T10 (plan.md split) dispatched. 4 agents in flight: m1-hardener, codex-fixer-1, codex-fixer-2, plan-splitter. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3505:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:192:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3506:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:193:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3507:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:194:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3508:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:195:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:11:### PD-001 — Doctrine vocabulary collision: D0–D5 vs RMP-bible invariants vs reactivity ADRs
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3509:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:196:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:13:**Decision (autonomous):** The canonical D0–D5 is what's in `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 (kernel-boundary, best-effort, negentropy-first, outbox-automatic, single-writer-per-fact, snapshots-bounded). I corrected the README to match (commit pending in next push).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3510:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:197:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:15:**Why:** Codex review of merge `51120cb` flagged that I had been using a different D2/D3/D5 mapping throughout this session — conflating the product-spec D0–D5 with RMP-bible invariants (errors-never-cross-FFI is invariant #2; capabilities-report-don't-decide is RMP cardinal rule #6; ≤60 Hz reactivity is from ADR-0001..0004). They're all real binding rules, but they're three different rule sources. I had collapsed them into a single "D0–D5" rubric, which was wrong.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3511:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:198:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:19:- Various codex review prompts I wrote earlier passed the wrong D0–D5 to codex. The flagged findings in those reviews are still valid (codex review the doctrine word-by-word), but the framing was off.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3512:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:199:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:20:- `docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md` — uses the canonical D0–D5 correctly (codex flagged + fixed earlier).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3513:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:200:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:22:**Open question for user:** Do you want me to (a) keep the canonical product-spec D0–D5 as the single rubric and downgrade the RMP-bible invariants + reactivity-ADR rules to "supporting rules" (cited by number where relevant); or (b) expand the canonical D0–D5 to incorporate the three additional load-bearing rules (would require editing `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 — that's the authoritative source so changing it is a meaningful commit).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3514:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:201:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:24:**Current default while you decide:** option (a). README + framework-magic docs will be aligned to product-spec D0–D5; supporting rules cited by their source (RMP-bible invariant N, ADR-NNNN, etc.).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3515:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:202:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3516:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:203:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:41:### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3518:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:205:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3521:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:210:docs/design/view-catalog/stubs-validation-next.md:56:- **Computed-from-native fields.** All formatting, all derivations live in Rust per doctrine D5.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3522:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:211:docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:103:- **Bare pubkey path.** If no kind:0 exists in the store yet, `open()` returns a placeholder-filled payload and the reverse-index dependency triggers `on_event_inserted` when one arrives. Doctrine D1 prohibits returning `None` here.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3523:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:212:docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:234:- **Author kind:0 not yet loaded.** Per doctrine D1, render the item with placeholders. The `by_author` reindex on `on_projection_changed` updates the item in place. **The post is rendered immediately.**

All share `View*` payload conventions: pre-formatted strings (per doctrine D1), no business logic in Swift, composite-keyed `ViewDependencies` (per ADR-0001).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3527:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:216:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3528:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:218:docs/design/podcast/podcast-feeds.md:122:All actions return parsed records. None of them write to the domain store directly — `podcast-core::SubscribePodcast`/`RefreshFeed` orchestrate the writes, preserving the doctrine: **one writer per fact** (D4).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3529:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:219:docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:85:Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3594:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:288:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:578:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3706:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:403:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2361:++> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3787:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1847:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6544:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:576:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3795:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1855:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6566:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:983:   380	- Doctrine review (D0–D5) signed off on the FFI surface in writing in `docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3796:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1856:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6578:+docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:215:- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3797:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1857:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6581:+docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3798:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1858:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6582:+docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:85:Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3854:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1914:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6696:+docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3864:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1924:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6726:+250:- the working device path here was `xcodebuild -showdestinations`, then `xcodebuild build` for device id `00008150-001E118E3CD2401C`, then `xcrun devicectl device install app`, then `xcrun devicectl device process launch --terminate-existing com.podcastr.next.Podcastr` [Task 2]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3865:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1925:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6727:+2304:- `xcodegen generate` is required here after adding new Swift files, and the working device path was `xcodebuild -project WeightTracker.xcodeproj -scheme WeightTracker -destination 'platform=iOS,id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' build` followed by `xcrun devicectl device install app` and `xcrun devicectl device process launch` [Task 4][Task 5]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3866:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1926:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6728:+2468:- the working iPhone path was `cargo build --target aarch64-apple-ios --release -p tenex-core`, then `xcodebuild -workspace TenexMVP.xcworkspace -scheme TenexMVP -configuration Debug -destination 'platform=iOS,id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' -derivedDataPath .codex-derived-ios-device -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .codex-derived-ios-device/SourcePackages build`, followed by `xcrun devicectl device install app` and `xcrun devicectl device process launch --terminate-existing com.tenex.mvp` [Task 1]

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3874:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1934:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6872:+   181	**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3875:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1935:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6933:+   242	Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3876:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1936:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7096:+    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3877:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1937:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7153:+     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3878:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1938:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7164:+    20	- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3879:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1940:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7209:+    65	4. **The doctrine rubric is final.** Every PR is reviewed against the cardinal doctrines (`product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D5). A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3880:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1941:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7210:+    66	5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3881:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1942:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7244:+   100	- ✅ Best-effort rendering (D1): placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3882:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1946:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7398:+    75	**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3883:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1947:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7600:+A few likely consistency issues are emerging: key-layout drift between the master doc and `keys.md`, trait methods named in summaries but absent from the actual trait, and a possible D2/working-set leak around the “open view cover” being outside the trait contract. I’m checking those against the exact sub-doc lines now.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4027:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2093:docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:41:C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4028:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2094:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4029:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2095:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:54:## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4030:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2096:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4031:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2097:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4032:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2098:docs/plan/m8-multi-account.md:7:**Scope.** Per spec doctrine D4 (single writer per fact) extended to account scope:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4033:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2099:docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:34:**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4034:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2102:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:17:NMP doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns; D1 best-effort placeholders; D2 reactivity ≤60Hz/view + working-set bound; D3 errors never cross FFI; D4 one writer per fact; D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4035:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2104:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:99:+- **Cardinal doctrine (D0–D5).** Every PR is reviewed against this rubric. A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4036:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2105:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:100:+  - **D0** Kernel never grows app nouns (proven by the M11 podcast rebuild and the existing fixture-todo-core)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4037:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2106:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:101:+  - **D1** Best-effort rendering: placeholders → in-place refinement, never withhold known data
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4038:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2107:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:102:+  - **D2** Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4039:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2108:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:103:+  - **D3** Errors never cross FFI — become `toast: Option<String>` state fields
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4040:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2109:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:104:+  - **D4** One writer per fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4041:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2110:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:105:+  - **D5** Capabilities report, never decide policy
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4042:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2111:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:162:+| `docs/product-spec.md` + `docs/product-spec/` | What we ship at v1. The cardinal doctrine D0–D5 lives in §1.5. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4043:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2112:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:175:+2. `docs/product-spec.md` §1.5 — the doctrine D0–D5.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4044:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2113:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:194:+> **Source directives:** `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md` "Framework magic contract" section; `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12; `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 (cardinal doctrines D0–D5) + §3.3 (bug-class extinction); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1–§7.8.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4045:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2115:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:223:+| C5 | Kind:3 auto-tracking: active account's follow-list change recompiles dependent subscriptions transparently | kind3.md | `c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs` | **[PENDING M2]** | scope-adj §"Folded into M2"; D3; M2 design §4 (Trigger::Nip65Arrived analog for kind:3) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4046:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2116:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:224:+| C6 | Outbox read routing: `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated | outbox.md | `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays` | **[PENDING M2]** | D3; spec §7.3 row "Subscription with `authors`"; M2 design §7 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4047:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2117:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:225:+| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4048:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2118:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:228:+| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[PENDING M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4049:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2119:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:230:+| C12 | Account switch is a state transition: dispatching the switch action re-resolves every `ActiveAccount`-scoped view without the app issuing CLOSE/REQ or rebuilding view handles | sessions.md | `c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance` | **[PENDING M8]** | D4; spec §7.4; §3.3 bug #5; M2 §4 trigger A4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4050:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2120:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:231:+| C13 | Best-effort rendering: every view payload field is non-`Option`; missing data uses defined placeholders (shortened npub, identicon, "just now"); the same payload updates in place when authoritative data arrives | capabilities.md | `c13_view_payload_uses_placeholders_then_refines_in_place` | **[DONE]** in placeholder shape; **[PENDING M2/M3]** for in-place refinement on enrich | D1; spec §7.6 "Best-effort field contract"; aim §4.12 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4051:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2121:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:293:+> Read first: `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 doctrine D1; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 (the per-field placeholder table + the `TimelineItem` concrete example); `docs/aim.md` §4.12; `docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4052:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2122:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:311:+**Failure mode prevented:** the entire class of "Nostr-client cold-start UI" bugs `subsystems.md` §1.5 D1 enumerates as ruled out by construction:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4053:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2123:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:318:+The bug-extinction surface in `overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 does not have a single numbered bug for this because the failures are UX defects rather than data-corruption bugs, but the doctrine clause D1 is the explicit promise the contract holds.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4054:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2124:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:344:+C13 is the canonical instance of cardinal doctrine **D1**. The doctrine clause's wording — *"There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API"* — is testable through the payload shape itself, which is what sub-path 1's "no `loading` field" assertion checks. The framework cannot guarantee the app does not implement its own spinner over the payload, but it can guarantee the API does not give the app a way to ask the question that would justify one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4055:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2125:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:346:+C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4056:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2126:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:387:+The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D5 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and onto the older `aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12. The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4057:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2127:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:391:+| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4058:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2128:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:392:+| **D1** best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | C13 (placeholders), C1–C4 (refinement triggers), C5 (kind:3 → re-render of follow-derived views) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4059:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2129:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:393:+| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4060:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2130:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:394:+| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4061:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2131:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:395:+| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4062:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2132:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:396:+| **D5** snapshots bounded by what's open | C8 (view-scoped subscriptions; buffered ≤60Hz per view) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4063:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2133:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:422:+1. **It forces honesty.** If a chapter cannot fill the "App writes" field with `"nothing"` or a single safe call, the framework has leaked the operation to the app, and the doctrine D0 boundary is violated. The author of that chapter is required to file an ADR rather than ship the bullet as-is.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4064:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2134:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:546:+Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4065:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2135:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:594:+## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4066:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2136:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:596:+C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4067:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2137:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:602:+The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4068:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2138:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:754:+C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4069:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2139:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:898:+The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4070:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2140:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:952:+**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4071:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2141:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1257: | 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4072:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2142:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1258: | 2026-05-18 02:10 | 2 | Heartbeat #2. Landed: T9 ffi-safety-commenter 03d7a23 (SAFETY comments accurate, semantics unchanged, cargo+tests pass; codex flagged pre-existing clippy lints in actor.rs+reactivity-bench → T12); m1-hardener live.rs prototype stashed (was polluting main tree again). Two outstanding codex reviews caught up: codex on 031fc07 found 8 substantive M2-design issues (no-publish-to-indexer, D4 single-writer, D0 module ownership, plan-id churn, lane modeling) → T13; codex on 9fead0e found 10 substantive M3-design issues (atomicity ADR blocker, D2 not enforced through trait, GC pinned-overlay unbounded, kind:5 a-tag deletes missing, tombstone semantics, key encoding mismatches, scan_by_kind_dtag missing from trait) → T14. Worktree agent-a41054fd04781ce06 cleaned. T10 (plan.md split) dispatched. 4 agents in flight: m1-hardener, codex-fixer-1, codex-fixer-2, plan-splitter. |
