docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4089:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2162:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3543:    39	| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[PENDING M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4090:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2163:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3545:    41	| C12 | Account switch is a state transition: dispatching the switch action re-resolves every `ActiveAccount`-scoped view without the app issuing CLOSE/REQ or rebuilding view handles | sessions.md | `c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance` | **[PENDING M8]** | D4; spec §7.4; §3.3 bug #5; M2 §4 trigger A4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4091:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2164:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3546:    42	| C13 | Best-effort rendering: every view payload field is non-`Option`; missing data uses defined placeholders (shortened npub, identicon, "just now"); the same payload updates in place when authoritative data arrives | capabilities.md | `c13_view_payload_uses_placeholders_then_refines_in_place` | **[DONE]** in placeholder shape; **[PENDING M2/M3]** for in-place refinement on enrich | D1; spec §7.6 "Best-effort field contract"; aim §4.12 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4092:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2165:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3614:    21	The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D5 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and onto the older `aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12. The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4093:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2166:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3618:    25	| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4094:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2167:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3619:    26	| **D1** best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | C13 (placeholders), C1–C4 (refinement triggers), C5 (kind:3 → re-render of follow-derived views) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4095:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2168:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3620:    27	| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4096:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2169:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3621:    28	| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4097:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2170:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3622:    29	| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4098:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2171:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3623:    30	| **D5** snapshots bounded by what's open | C8 (view-scoped subscriptions; buffered ≤60Hz per view) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4099:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2172:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3638:/bin/zsh -lc 'rg -n "D0|D1|D2|D3|D4|D5|outbox routing is automatic|Capabilities report|capabilities report|Errors never cross" docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md docs/aim.md docs/design/framework-magic docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md README.md' in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4100:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2173:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3640:README.md:25:- **Cardinal doctrine (D0–D5).** Every PR is reviewed against this rubric. A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4101:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2174:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3641:README.md:26:  - **D0** Kernel never grows app nouns (proven by the M11 podcast rebuild and the existing fixture-todo-core)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4102:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2175:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3642:README.md:27:  - **D1** Best-effort rendering: placeholders → in-place refinement, never withhold known data
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4103:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2176:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3643:README.md:28:  - **D2** Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4104:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2177:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3644:README.md:29:  - **D3** Errors never cross FFI — become `toast: Option<String>` state fields
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4105:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2178:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3645:README.md:30:  - **D4** One writer per fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4106:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2179:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3646:README.md:31:  - **D5** Capabilities report, never decide policy
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4107:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2180:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3647:README.md:88:| `docs/product-spec.md` + `docs/product-spec/` | What we ship at v1. The cardinal doctrine D0–D5 lives in §1.5. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4108:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2181:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3648:README.md:101:2. `docs/product-spec.md` §1.5 — the doctrine D0–D5.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4109:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2182:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3649:docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:34:The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4110:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2183:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3650:docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:41:C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4111:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2184:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3651:docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:34:**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4112:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2185:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3652:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:21:The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D5 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and onto the older `aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12. The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4113:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2186:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3653:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:25:| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4114:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2187:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3654:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:26:| **D1** best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | C13 (placeholders), C1–C4 (refinement triggers), C5 (kind:3 → re-render of follow-derived views) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4115:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2188:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3655:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:27:| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4116:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2189:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3656:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:28:| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4117:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2190:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3657:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:29:| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4118:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2191:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3658:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:30:| **D5** snapshots bounded by what's open | C8 (view-scoped subscriptions; buffered ≤60Hz per view) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4119:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2192:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3659:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:56:1. **It forces honesty.** If a chapter cannot fill the "App writes" field with `"nothing"` or a single safe call, the framework has leaked the operation to the app, and the doctrine D0 boundary is violated. The author of that chapter is required to file an ADR rather than ship the bullet as-is.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4120:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2193:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3660:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4121:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2194:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3661:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:54:## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4122:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2195:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3662:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4123:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2196:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3663:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4124:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2197:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3664:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:4:> Read first: `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 doctrine D1; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 (the per-field placeholder table + the `TimelineItem` concrete example); `docs/aim.md` §4.12; `docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4125:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2198:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3665:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:22:**Failure mode prevented:** the entire class of "Nostr-client cold-start UI" bugs `subsystems.md` §1.5 D1 enumerates as ruled out by construction:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4126:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2199:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3666:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:29:The bug-extinction surface in `overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 does not have a single numbered bug for this because the failures are UX defects rather than data-corruption bugs, but the doctrine clause D1 is the explicit promise the contract holds.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4127:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2200:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3667:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:55:C13 is the canonical instance of cardinal doctrine **D1**. The doctrine clause's wording — *"There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API"* — is testable through the payload shape itself, which is what sub-path 1's "no `loading` field" assertion checks. The framework cannot guarantee the app does not implement its own spinner over the payload, but it can guarantee the API does not give the app a way to ask the question that would justify one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4128:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2201:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3668:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:57:C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4155:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2231:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4082:    77	### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4156:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2232:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4196:   181	**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4157:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2233:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4326:    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4158:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2234:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4629:     4	> Read first: `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 doctrine D1; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 (the per-field placeholder table + the `TimelineItem` concrete example); `docs/aim.md` §4.12; `docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4159:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2235:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4647:    22	**Failure mode prevented:** the entire class of "Nostr-client cold-start UI" bugs `subsystems.md` §1.5 D1 enumerates as ruled out by construction:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4160:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2236:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4654:    29	The bug-extinction surface in `overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 does not have a single numbered bug for this because the failures are UX defects rather than data-corruption bugs, but the doctrine clause D1 is the explicit promise the contract holds.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4161:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2237:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4680:    55	C13 is the canonical instance of cardinal doctrine **D1**. The doctrine clause's wording — *"There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API"* — is testable through the payload shape itself, which is what sub-path 1's "no `loading` field" assertion checks. The framework cannot guarantee the app does not implement its own spinner over the payload, but it can guarantee the API does not give the app a way to ask the question that would justify one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4162:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2238:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4682:    57	C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4163:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2239:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:5163:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:4:> Read first: `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 doctrine D1; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 (the per-field placeholder table + the `TimelineItem` concrete example); `docs/aim.md` §4.12; `docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md`.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4278:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:344:+C13 is the canonical instance of cardinal doctrine **D1**. The doctrine clause's wording — *"There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API"* — is testable through the payload shape itself, which is what sub-path 1's "no `loading` field" assertion checks. The framework cannot guarantee the app does not implement its own spinner over the payload, but it can guarantee the API does not give the app a way to ask the question that would justify one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4279:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:346:+C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4284:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:387:+The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D5 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and onto the older `aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12. The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4286:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:391:+| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4287:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:392:+| **D1** best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | C13 (placeholders), C1–C4 (refinement triggers), C5 (kind:3 → re-render of follow-derived views) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4288:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:393:+| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4289:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:394:+| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4290:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:395:+| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4291:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:396:+| **D5** snapshots bounded by what's open | C8 (view-scoped subscriptions; buffered ≤60Hz per view) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4293:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:422:+1. **It forces honesty.** If a chapter cannot fill the "App writes" field with `"nothing"` or a single safe call, the framework has leaked the operation to the app, and the doctrine D0 boundary is violated. The author of that chapter is required to file an ADR rather than ship the bullet as-is.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4300:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:546:+Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4302:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:594:+## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4303:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:596:+C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4304:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:602:+The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4314:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:754:+C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4328:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:898:+The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4329:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:952:+**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4331:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1257: | 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

Two outstanding codex reviews caught up: codex on 031fc07 found 8 substantive M2-design issues (no-publish-to-indexer, D4 single-writer, D0 module ownership, plan-id churn, lane modeling) → T13; codex on 9fead0e found 10 substantive M3-design issues (atomicity ADR blocker, D2 not enforced through trait, GC pinned-overlay unbounded, kind:5 a-tag deletes missing, tombstone semantics, key encoding mismatches, scan_by_kind_dtag missing from trait) → T14. Worktree agent-a41054fd04781ce06 cleaned. T10 (plan.md split) dispatched. 4 agents in flight: m1-hardener, codex-fixer-1, codex-fixer-2, plan-splitter. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4406:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3662:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4407:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3663:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4408:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3664:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:4:> Read first: `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 doctrine D1; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 (the per-field placeholder table + the `TimelineItem` concrete example); `docs/aim.md` §4.12; `docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4409:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3665:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:22:**Failure mode prevented:** the entire class of "Nostr-client cold-start UI" bugs `subsystems.md` §1.5 D1 enumerates as ruled out by construction:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4410:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3666:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:29:The bug-extinction surface in `overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 does not have a single numbered bug for this because the failures are UX defects rather than data-corruption bugs, but the doctrine clause D1 is the explicit promise the contract holds.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4411:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3667:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:55:C13 is the canonical instance of cardinal doctrine **D1**. The doctrine clause's wording — *"There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API"* — is testable through the payload shape itself, which is what sub-path 1's "no `loading` field" assertion checks. The framework cannot guarantee the app does not implement its own spinner over the payload, but it can guarantee the API does not give the app a way to ask the question that would justify one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4412:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3668:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:57:C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4423:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3720:    41	### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4425:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3733:    54	### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4426:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3739:    60	### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4427:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3759:     6	Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4429:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3807:    54	## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4430:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3809:    56	C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4431:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3815:    62	The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4433:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3860:    34	The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4434:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3912:    34	**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4438:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3987:    39	- Profile placeholders → in-place refinement (D1; already done)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4441:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4045:README.md:28:  - **D2** Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4442:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4046:README.md:29:  - **D3** Errors never cross FFI — become `toast: Option<String>` state fields
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4443:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4047:README.md:31:  - **D5** Capabilities report, never decide policy

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4455:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4196:   181	**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4456:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4326:    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4462:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4629:     4	> Read first: `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 doctrine D1; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 (the per-field placeholder table + the `TimelineItem` concrete example); `docs/aim.md` §4.12; `docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4466:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4647:    22	**Failure mode prevented:** the entire class of "Nostr-client cold-start UI" bugs `subsystems.md` §1.5 D1 enumerates as ruled out by construction:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4467:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4654:    29	The bug-extinction surface in `overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 does not have a single numbered bug for this because the failures are UX defects rather than data-corruption bugs, but the doctrine clause D1 is the explicit promise the contract holds.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4471:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4680:    55	C13 is the canonical instance of cardinal doctrine **D1**. The doctrine clause's wording — *"There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API"* — is testable through the payload shape itself, which is what sub-path 1's "no `loading` field" assertion checks. The framework cannot guarantee the app does not implement its own spinner over the payload, but it can guarantee the API does not give the app a way to ask the question that would justify one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4472:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4682:    57	C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4586:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:21:The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D5 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and onto the older `aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12. The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4588:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:25:| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4589:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:26:| **D1** best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | C13 (placeholders), C1–C4 (refinement triggers), C5 (kind:3 → re-render of follow-derived views) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4590:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:27:| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4591:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:28:| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4592:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:29:| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4593:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:30:| **D5** snapshots bounded by what's open | C8 (view-scoped subscriptions; buffered ≤60Hz per view) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4595:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:56:1. **It forces honesty.** If a chapter cannot fill the "App writes" field with `"nothing"` or a single safe call, the framework has leaked the operation to the app, and the doctrine D0 boundary is violated. The author of that chapter is required to file an ADR rather than ship the bullet as-is.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4617:docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:41:C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4621:docs/perf/codex-reviews/b4b6afa.md:3:**Merge:** docs(m11): fix codex feedback (file sizes, harness, D5, D4, rig.rs gate, OPML, counts)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4622:docs/plan/principles.md:8:4. **The doctrine rubric is final.** Every PR is reviewed against the cardinal doctrines (`product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D5). A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4623:docs/plan/principles.md:9:5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the [M11](m11-podcast.md) podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4624:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4626:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:54:## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4627:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4628:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4646:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:66:| 8 | `nmp_app_open_author(*mut, *const c_char)` | `void nmp_app_open_author(void *app, const char *pubkey)` | UTF-8 C string, expected 64-char lowercase hex pubkey. Hex-validated via `is_hex_pubkey`. Trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace. Empty / non-hex inputs are **silently dropped** (see §7 finding D3-gap). | Silent no-op on null app or null pubkey. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4682:docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:34:The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4683:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:15:You are reviewing merge 0dfb97581315aa04bd66341a98a32215de5e14d6 (the M11 podcast-app rebuild design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4703:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1767:+All actions return parsed records. None of them write to the domain store directly — `podcast-core::SubscribePodcast`/`RefreshFeed` orchestrate the writes, preserving the doctrine: **one writer per fact** (D4).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4704:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:2087:+- Failure-mode test: capability emits `Error { reason: "rate_limit" }` mid-stream → `ChatTurn` commits with `is_error: true` and the `toast` field is set per doctrine D3.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4712:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3037:docs/plan.md:20:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4720:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3493:A few issues are now concrete rather than stylistic: the design has at least one hard LOC conflict with the copy-verbatim requirement, and the screenshot gate currently depends on an agent-only UI introspection path. I’m pulling the exact lines for those plus the doctrine/D4 ownership concerns.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4724:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3720:    85	### Bounded-state proof (D5)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4725:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3978:3. [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:79): `AudioPlaybackBridge` decides repeated `Load` is a no-op based on `currentEpisode`. That is playback policy in native, and it conflicts with the same doc’s D5 claim at [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:87) plus the lesson at [lessons.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:58). Fix: Rust decides whether to send `Load`; the bridge only executes requests and reports state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4726:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3980:4. [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:130), [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:231), and [podcast-rag.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:132): `podcast-core` stores `embedding_id`, says it is re-exported from `podcast-rag`, while `podcast-rag` depends on `podcast-core` and backfills the core record. That creates either a crate cycle or a D4 one-writer violation. Fix: define `EmbeddingId` in `podcast-core` or a shared types crate, and have core own any writes to core records after `podcast-rag` returns `Indexed`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4727:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3997:3. [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:79): `AudioPlaybackBridge` decides repeated `Load` is a no-op based on `currentEpisode`. That is playback policy in native, and it conflicts with the same doc’s D5 claim at [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:87) plus the lesson at [lessons.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:58). Fix: Rust decides whether to send `Load`; the bridge only executes requests and reports state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4728:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3999:4. [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:130), [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:231), and [podcast-rag.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:132): `podcast-core` stores `embedding_id`, says it is re-exported from `podcast-rag`, while `podcast-rag` depends on `podcast-core` and backfills the core record. That creates either a crate cycle or a D4 one-writer violation. Fix: define `EmbeddingId` in `podcast-core` or a shared types crate, and have core own any writes to core records after `podcast-rag` returns `Indexed`.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4826:docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:827:d660735:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:81:The doctrines from `product-spec.md` §1.5 (D1 best-effort rendering, D2 negentropy first, D3 outbox automatic, D4 single writer per fact, D5 snapshots bounded by what's open) survive intact and apply across all modules.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4837:docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:948:d660735:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4841:docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1032:d660735:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:54:### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4845:docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1055:d660735:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4846:docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1058:d660735:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4868:docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1622:- `docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:272-282`: D0 is incorrectly marked compliant. The doc itself cites `Profile` and `TimelineItem` inside `nmp-core`, and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:57-114` contains `Profile`, `TimelineItem`, `ProfileCard`, `AuthorViewPayload`, and `ThreadViewPayload`. Per ADR-0009, `nmp-core` must not contain Profile/Timeline/Thread view-kind business logic. Fix the audit to mark this as blocking D0 debt or move those nouns out before signoff.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4869:docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1624:- `docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:299-351`: D2, D3, and D5 audit the wrong doctrines. They cover “reactivity contract,” “errors never cross FFI,” and “capabilities report,” but the stated doctrines are negentropy-first, automatic outbox routing, and snapshots bounded by open views. Fix those sections and remove the “all doctrines compliant” claims at `:410`, `:422`, and `:430` until the real doctrines are audited.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4870:docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1633:- `docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:272-282`: D0 is incorrectly marked compliant. The doc itself cites `Profile` and `TimelineItem` inside `nmp-core`, and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:57-114` contains `Profile`, `TimelineItem`, `ProfileCard`, `AuthorViewPayload`, and `ThreadViewPayload`. Per ADR-0009, `nmp-core` must not contain Profile/Timeline/Thread view-kind business logic. Fix the audit to mark this as blocking D0 debt or move those nouns out before signoff.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4871:docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1635:- `docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:299-351`: D2, D3, and D5 audit the wrong doctrines. They cover “reactivity contract,” “errors never cross FFI,” and “capabilities report,” but the stated doctrines are negentropy-first, automatic outbox routing, and snapshots bounded by open views. Fix those sections and remove the “all doctrines compliant” claims at `:410`, `:422`, and `:430` until the real doctrines are audited.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4873:docs/plan/m8-multi-account.md:7:**Scope.** Per spec doctrine D4 (single writer per fact) extended to account scope:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4876:docs/plan/m10-blossom.md:23:- Profile picture update through compose → kind:0 republish with new Blossom URL → in-place refinement across all open Profile / Timeline payloads (per doctrine D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4877:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:15:You are reviewing merge edc17b0 (design(framework-magic): contract + 10 sub-files + test-scaffolding (task #17)) on master in nostr-multi-platform. This adds the framework-magic design contract covering kind:3 auto-tracking, bunker:// signer, new-nsec flow, and 14 named behavioral tests. Doctrine D0-D5:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4878:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:16:- D0: kernel never grows app nouns (nmp-core stays substrate-only; per ADR-0009)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4879:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:17:- D1: best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place; no spinners gating renderable content

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4884:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:54:+> **Source directives:** `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md` "Framework magic contract" section; `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12; `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 (cardinal doctrines D0–D5) + §3.3 (bug-class extinction); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1–§7.8.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4889:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:83:+| C5 | Kind:3 auto-tracking: active account's follow-list change recompiles dependent subscriptions transparently | kind3.md | `c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs` | **[PENDING M2]** | scope-adj §"Folded into M2"; D3; M2 design §4 (Trigger::Nip65Arrived analog for kind:3) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4890:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:84:+| C6 | Outbox read routing: `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated | outbox.md | `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays` | **[PENDING M2]** | D3; spec §7.3 row "Subscription with `authors`"; M2 design §7 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4891:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:85:+| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4893:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:88:+| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[PENDING M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4894:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:90:+| C12 | Account switch is a state transition: dispatching the switch action re-resolves every `ActiveAccount`-scoped view without the app issuing CLOSE/REQ or rebuilding view handles | sessions.md | `c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance` | **[PENDING M8]** | D4; spec §7.4; §3.3 bug #5; M2 §4 trigger A4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4895:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:91:+| C13 | Best-effort rendering: every view payload field is non-`Option`; missing data uses defined placeholders (shortened npub, identicon, "just now"); the same payload updates in place when authoritative data arrives | capabilities.md | `c13_view_payload_uses_placeholders_then_refines_in_place` | **[DONE]** in placeholder shape; **[PENDING M2/M3]** for in-place refinement on enrich | D1; spec §7.6 "Best-effort field contract"; aim §4.12 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4897:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:153:+> Read first: `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 doctrine D1; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 (the per-field placeholder table + the `TimelineItem` concrete example); `docs/aim.md` §4.12; `docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4901:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:171:+**Failure mode prevented:** the entire class of "Nostr-client cold-start UI" bugs `subsystems.md` §1.5 D1 enumerates as ruled out by construction:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4902:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:178:+The bug-extinction surface in `overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 does not have a single numbered bug for this because the failures are UX defects rather than data-corruption bugs, but the doctrine clause D1 is the explicit promise the contract holds.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4906:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:204:+C13 is the canonical instance of cardinal doctrine **D1**. The doctrine clause's wording — *"There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API"* — is testable through the payload shape itself, which is what sub-path 1's "no `loading` field" assertion checks. The framework cannot guarantee the app does not implement its own spinner over the payload, but it can guarantee the API does not give the app a way to ask the question that would justify one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4907:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:206:+C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4912:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:247:+The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D5 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and onto the older `aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12. The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4914:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:251:+| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4915:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:252:+| **D1** best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | C13 (placeholders), C1–C4 (refinement triggers), C5 (kind:3 → re-render of follow-derived views) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4916:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:253:+| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4917:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:254:+| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4918:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:255:+| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4919:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:256:+| **D5** snapshots bounded by what's open | C8 (view-scoped subscriptions; buffered ≤60Hz per view) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4921:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:282:+1. **It forces honesty.** If a chapter cannot fill the "App writes" field with `"nothing"` or a single safe call, the framework has leaked the operation to the app, and the doctrine D0 boundary is violated. The author of that chapter is required to file an ADR rather than ship the bullet as-is.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4928:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:406:+Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4930:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:454:+## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4931:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:456:+C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4932:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:462:+The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4942:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:614:+C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4956:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:758:+The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4957:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:812:+**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4959:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:1060:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance — does the framework-magic contract violate D0 by adding app nouns to the kernel, (2) TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking in, (3) file-size budget per file, (4) internal consistency of the contract (are the 14 tests coherent with the contract). Be terse. Name file:line for any real concern.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4961:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:4:> Substrate reference: [`../kernel-substrate.md`](../kernel-substrate.md) §5; doctrine: D5 (capabilities report, never decide).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4969:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:22:**Failure mode prevented:** the entire class of "Nostr-client cold-start UI" bugs `subsystems.md` §1.5 D1 enumerates as ruled out by construction:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4970:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:29:The bug-extinction surface in `overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 does not have a single numbered bug for this because the failures are UX defects rather than data-corruption bugs, but the doctrine clause D1 is the explicit promise the contract holds.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4974:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:55:C13 is the canonical instance of cardinal doctrine **D1**. The doctrine clause's wording — *"There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API"* — is testable through the payload shape itself, which is what sub-path 1's "no `loading` field" assertion checks. The framework cannot guarantee the app does not implement its own spinner over the payload, but it can guarantee the API does not give the app a way to ask the question that would justify one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4975:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:57:C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4977:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:15:You are reviewing merge 031fc07 (M2 subscription compilation + outbox routing design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity contract, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4981:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:547:+- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5020:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1750:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5021:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1751:docs/plan.md:20:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5108:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4387:- [nip65.md:192](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:192) plus [nip65.md:197](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:197): `nmp-nip65` depends on `nmp-core`, while `nmp-core::kernel::planner` consumes `nmp-nip65`. That is a Rust crate cycle and blurs D0. Fix by moving mailbox traits/types into `nmp-core` or a tiny no-core crate, with `nmp-nip65` only registering module behavior.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5109:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4389:- [nip65.md:6](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:6), [nip65.md:98](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:98), [compiler.md:52](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:52): mailbox facts have too many apparent writers/read models: `ingest_relay_list`, `MailboxCache`, and `MailboxesView::on_event_inserted`. D4 wants one writer per fact. Fix by making one canonical mailbox projection/cache writer; views only read that projection.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5122:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:15:You are reviewing merge 9fead0e (M3 LMDB schema + EventStore trait + GC policy design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5. File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5126:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:126:+**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5138:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1159:+## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5139:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1165:+- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5144:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1388:Review: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 + D2 — composite reverse index + working-set bound must remain enforced through the trait); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance; (4) consistency of EventStore trait surface across the 6 sub-docs; (5) the choice of nostr-lmdb as on-disk byte store vs roll-our-own — is the gap analysis honest?; (6) the GC eviction algorithm + budget; (7) the migration plumbing's testability; (8) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5173:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1448:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:15:You are reviewing merge 031fc07 (M2 subscription compilation + outbox routing design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity contract, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5174:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1449:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:547:+- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5175:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1450:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1112:+- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5176:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1451:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1567:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 — does nmp-nip65 propose any kernel-side type leaks? D4 — single source for relay-set facts?); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?); (4) internal consistency across the 8 sub-docs; (5) compatibility with the upcoming M3 LMDB schema (e.g. watermark table); (6) the planned test (m2_subscription_compilation_audit) exit criteria; (7) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5205:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1498:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4387:- [nip65.md:192](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:192) plus [nip65.md:197](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:197): `nmp-nip65` depends on `nmp-core`, while `nmp-core::kernel::planner` consumes `nmp-nip65`. That is a Rust crate cycle and blurs D0. Fix by moving mailbox traits/types into `nmp-core` or a tiny no-core crate, with `nmp-nip65` only registering module behavior.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5206:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1499:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4389:- [nip65.md:6](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:6), [nip65.md:98](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:98), [compiler.md:52](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:52): mailbox facts have too many apparent writers/read models: `ingest_relay_list`, `MailboxCache`, and `MailboxesView::on_event_inserted`. D4 wants one writer per fact. Fix by making one canonical mailbox projection/cache writer; views only read that projection.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5294:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1606:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2361:++> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5372:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1750:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3912:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5373:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1751:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3913:docs/plan.md:20:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5374:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1752:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3914:docs/plan.md:65:4. **The doctrine rubric is final.** Every PR is reviewed against the cardinal doctrines (`product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D5). docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5444:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1823:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3987:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:77:### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5445:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1824:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3990:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5446:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1825:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3991:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5447:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1826:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3992:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5448:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1827:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4005:     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5473:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1855:docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:103:- **Bare pubkey path.** If no kind:0 exists in the store yet, `open()` returns a placeholder-filled payload and the reverse-index dependency triggers `on_event_inserted` when one arrives. Doctrine D1 prohibits returning `None` here.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5474:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1856:docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:234:- **Author kind:0 not yet loaded.** Per doctrine D1, render the item with placeholders. The `by_author` reindex on `on_projection_changed` updates the item in place. **The post is rendered immediately.**
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5475:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1868:docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:94:**Exit gate.** Cold launch with primed LMDB renders the seed-driven timeline in ≤ 1.5s, showing fresh content from hundreds of authors followed by the seeds; tap an author → profile screen → back works; kind:0 arriving mid-scroll updates all author rows in place per doctrine D1; updating one seed's kind:3 mid-session re-resolves the timeline's author set without manual intervention; reactivity-bench `--standard` continues to pass at the larger author-set size; firehose-bench `live` for `sustained_firehose` (running at the real seed-author scale, not modeled) lands within budgets.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5476:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1873:docs/design/podcast/inventory.md:65:| `Utilities/ErrorPresentation.swift` | 58 | swift | `ios/NmpPodcast/Bridge/ErrorPresentation.swift` | UI-only — `AppError`/`ErrorHandler` map `toast: Option<String>` from `AppState` (doctrine D3). No business logic. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5477:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1874:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:240:17 `ViewModule`s. Each in `podcast-core/src/views/<name>.rs`. All share `View*` payload conventions: pre-formatted strings (per doctrine D1), no business logic in Swift, composite-keyed `ViewDependencies` (per ADR-0001).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5479:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1890:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:15:You are reviewing merge 9fead0e (M3 LMDB schema + EventStore trait + GC policy design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5. File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5481:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1912:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:126:+**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5483:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1942:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1159:+## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5484:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1943:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1165:+- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5485:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1951:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1388:Review: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 + D2 — composite reverse index + working-set bound must remain enforced through the trait); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance; (4) consistency of EventStore trait surface across the 6 sub-docs; (5) the choice of nostr-lmdb as on-disk byte store vs roll-our-own — is the gap analysis honest?; (6) the GC eviction algorithm + budget; (7) the migration plumbing's testability; (8) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5503:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1986:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3037:docs/plan.md:20:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5504:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1987:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3041:docs/plan.md:66:5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5505:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1998:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3493:A few issues are now concrete rather than stylistic: the design has at least one hard LOC conflict with the copy-verbatim requirement, and the screenshot gate currently depends on an agent-only UI introspection path. I’m pulling the exact lines for those plus the doctrine/D4 ownership concerns.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5506:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1999:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3720:    85	### Bounded-state proof (D5)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5507:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2001:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3978:3. [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:79): `AudioPlaybackBridge` decides repeated `Load` is a no-op based on `currentEpisode`. That is playback policy in native, and it conflicts with the same doc’s D5 claim at [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:87) plus the lesson at [lessons.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:58). Fix: Rust decides whether to send `Load`; the bridge only executes requests and reports state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5508:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2002:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3980:4. [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:130), [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:231), and [podcast-rag.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:132): `podcast-core` stores `embedding_id`, says it is re-exported from `podcast-rag`, while `podcast-rag` depends on `podcast-core` and backfills the core record. That creates either a crate cycle or a D4 one-writer violation. Fix: define `EmbeddingId` in `podcast-core` or a shared types crate, and have core own any writes to core records after `podcast-rag` returns `Indexed`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5509:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2003:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3997:3. [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:79): `AudioPlaybackBridge` decides repeated `Load` is a no-op based on `currentEpisode`. That is playback policy in native, and it conflicts with the same doc’s D5 claim at [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:87) plus the lesson at [lessons.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:58). Fix: Rust decides whether to send `Load`; the bridge only executes requests and reports state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5510:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2004:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3999:4. [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:130), [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:231), and [podcast-rag.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:132): `podcast-core` stores `embedding_id`, says it is re-exported from `podcast-rag`, while `podcast-rag` depends on `podcast-core` and backfills the core record. That creates either a crate cycle or a D4 one-writer violation. Fix: define `EmbeddingId` in `podcast-core` or a shared types crate, and have core own any writes to core records after `podcast-rag` returns `Indexed`.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5514:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2029:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5515:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2030:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5516:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2031:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
